## Senate Advances Shutdown Bill
The [Senate voted 60-40 on Sunday night](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO4yAQxvGnwZ0jGIyxC4pr3OXaKy1iBsMthggmifL2K-9qn2DbGekn_b9_1_VqY4p5_2sPNOTLmvHVEhJhXVt4kCuvfKtoPyjU8thDe9yxrsBB9UL0gq_OxvReh84ZjwgoOzRCj6A1jHrqghm9mMYJN4nSO6E3dbOTB-02C9IpPnTRnJoQggvBB6kvsxv8iFrMTnm8KcUGji5SqZf_JWYK6NO7PLFetnJ0yQSie2PyD4OFwUIBQ0zp_DFYQjnwDGKwNMyWkMGi1KxnLfvvQ_8shK3fTzAfmKn_qe4d2sRg6apx91dMKbZRs4Hvh41ffke_Guxp4DMAAP__htSBIg) to advance a legislative measure, paving the way to end the 40-day federal government shutdown and potentially reopen the government as early as this week. [Eight Democrats joined Republicans](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLESnCAURb8GOxwfoqwFRRq7TZvSeQsPJUFwANfZv8-4M_mC9Hfuuff8ei5P9MHH9SfupKtLS6SrBKqV8lK2s9p0xVcm_FO3nM51K-dBeRGdGDgAh26x6MNnkY3VjkhQ35AGNQqlxKgezaalMPAwZOVgaHKIiE5OMD16GBU6ZRqv7zYA6AA62at2stKNpGCyg6PXMDDZkfU15fZ38rFu5MInvSm3Ju1N0FutR2H9DyZmJubrutrvpOLXiOHOMDHfBCZmACbmbmJi_l7yceWZjvMVvMFYuKU9mYy18ExoNm4JA6do-T8RPFM6KPL1xu8UKxNzk7U9Lh-CL6Nislt39F9sU__L51uLvwEAAP__rfSMJw) to advance the measure, while a number of other Democrats, citing the lack of guaranteed Affordable Care Act (ACA) extension within the bill, voted no. The measure secures a future vote on extending expiring ACA subsidies, guarantees back pay for federal workers, and blocks further layoffs. The resulting compromise package [provides full fiscal-year funding](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkM9q7CAchZ_G7Az-S7xZuJhLCd1MoXTRZXD0l2hrNKiZMH36kkKfoLsDBw7n-96v01X74OPyoldQdU5ThKMEqBXyVNxebTriLYP-rC6nfXFl3yBPjLAOU4opmaz24TGJxqoZgAFvQFHZMylZL_81TjEyyH4wlAsORnBhDNdwMwzmjkkjROPVuUYpJZQSwWU7WDH3IOlguxluXYcEAetryu1H8rE6mMMj3SG3Jq1NUK7WrSB-QWxEbDyOo11LPDvERoh4L4iNJxRi45aCr96csUDUFXAGbRwUbEEHnCKGaH1ccHWAf-kRG3XGlwt97Z-_COJjMt4i_vT_tAZHeYO8NVnZ7fAh-NJLJMiyah9-_tU_Sb0r9h0AAP__wZ6M2A) for military construction, veterans' affairs, agriculture, and the legislative branch, while funding the remainder of the government through Jan. 30, 2026.
## BBC Heads Resign Over Doctored Trump Speech
The Director-General of the BBC, Tim Davie, and the CEO of BBC News, Deborah Turness, [both resigned Sunday](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2upDAMBeCnCZ1HOPzkUlBsQ3e33RIZYkhmQ4ISM6N5-xX7Cre29emc8-d7_iYffNx_08GjbGmO_C6BRTjPxV1i0zsumemvuJyu3ZXr5DzrWneACFjPlnz4zG1lx41Zc1PxiKbXxujefFVuJGyGof8aBtN2vCLqjQy13CDxQmRN5cdbQ8QasW4b8xhsu_VscLDdxkvXqbZm6yXlxzP5KI638Ekvzo81HVUYnchZVPNL6UnpKX7OVOQ-KT3dsNITotJTPSg9vVMOFu6KSk_LsoJjsiD-AEsvz5C5-D0WuHmwaZWU2cKWktDOkDaQfB1ngSdF6KGczKsDH-_X6-AolD9KT1Ue7fn2IfjSG9XW-0E-_E8rP5r4Nep_AQAA__8JcpGk) amid a growing scandal over the selective editing of a Donald Trump speech in the BBC's flagship investigative program, _Panorama_. Davie took "ultimate responsibility" for "mistakes made" at the public broadcaster after a leaked internal memo alleged the program used spliced clips from Trump's Jan. 6, 2021, speech to falsely suggest he called supporters to "fight like hell," omitting his actual call to "peacefully and patriotically make your voices heard." Compare [the original and the doctored clip here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL3OnTAQRJ_GdCB2wfijcBEportpUyL_LNiJwcgsl9y3j8grpBtppNGc8_M1v0xMcV9_mI00L3ne6T4TMVOZz3Cxz_duC5nfHEq-1nBeB5UZW5Q1QA3t7E1Mn7mvvF6IkLqKNKgBlcJBfVVBj2ilGtxibP9FCF2rsBuMHKSV6O1oq6ifNQBoAdq-U83o-2UgBaOXC1kpRd-Sj5xL8yvHnQMt6ZPfVBqXtyrpwHycovsmcBI43ffdfEzI-WkFTg-OwMkUji7RE-mPS9cZ31Rb62qfHedCvuZybUd9HkQu1CBhRAlKNoG3JLppvZzL185URPcdqqL9cceU4jko0bfrZmL6d4f_y-Fb498AAAD__6FJhsY). The whistleblower's memo also revealed that the documentary showed footage of people marching on the Capitol, which was, in fact, recorded an hour before Trump even began his remarks, creating the misleading impression that the march was a direct response to his speech. [Follow live updates here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL3OnDAURJ_GdCDfa4PZwsUnRXSbNiXyz_XirBcT7F1Cnj4ir5B6NKM558d9vpuY4vr4bl6ka8jzSkdJVCvtc1ne1edjtTuZZ132_H4s5b3RPiPHvgVogc_exHTOsvE6ECGJhjSoAZXCQY3Nos0oyBkZpB28DVINxvJRIKjeGuesaKK-1gCAA3ApVHfzMgyk4Ob7QLbvmeTkY8179zPHtS4U0pk_tHcuv5qkl1q3wsQXw4nhdBxHZ627MobTBcNwSvFDDCfnb89ff-A8fz8rE9OWS2XimymFKsNefIGQEseRt8EJbGUA1xoFQyvl6DnIEIIPDMXVa3bttyOmFMugmOSPl4np36H6XxY_Gv8GAAD__4SdhEc).
## Mysterious 'Fedora Man' at Louvre Unmasked
The mystery man spotted outside the Louvre after last month’s $102 million jewel heist—[dressed in a fedora, vest, and tie](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGSpCAYBOCnwYwp-UUZA4JLyObSCy30_1FuUSxA3dmn33JfYeOu-qq7_72Gl_XBb_Nfu5IuLg4bXTlQKZSGvBwF47WNiexHWVI85iUfO6UBami5EFzUA1of3oOsUDsioKYiLVQHSkGnntWi3TR2amrcKCbZytbaCTs59gi2x2cLfeX1rQkhaiFq2ahHj9J1pESPraOxbZmsCX2J6fE_-q0s5MI7npQeU1yroJdS9syaPwwMA7O995jLHTEwN8zACMHA1D0Dc8UUkN8TGZgQjzNR5o4wJstXu3GPyG3mO2GKnIK3mc82fcWNI4XTHp8MTJU07pcPwedOMVnPq_Xhp0v51YGnhu8AAAD__-99hYc)—has been revealed as a 15-year-old boy. Pedro Elias Garzon Delvaux, a French cosplayer, was visiting the museum with his parents when the viral photo, taken just after the robbery, was snapped. It sparked online buzz as people mistook him for a stylish detective or a possible insider. [See the photo here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAURb9GOybwBMGCYpvpZtstDfoeA7soBnHM_P3G_YUtb-5Nbs75eowPF1Ncn59uIVt9Hlc690S1Uhn3cFTM5zoVcj81lHw8w35sVEbgoJgQTPARXUzvUbZoPRFQ15IVugetodemDRY4mNkrIQ15zZEmUB12gIMiAKOGNl4TJYTgQnDZ6duA0vekxYDK06RUIzlhrLncvnNcayCf3vlF5TbnpU021LrtTffRwL2Bu9sugKu6QqlxTtTAPeXjVYh5wlwcW9zK3Ma2kGtmM0c1GKnJK2-63vFZuVkOSGbyXkrRFovbGVOKe68byZ-Li-nvu_5L2MvCbwAAAP__LK5-vw). In reality, Delvaux says he was unaware of the theft and just happened to be in the right place at the right time, wearing the right outfit. A fan of Sherlock Holmes and Hercule Poirot, Delvaux often wears vintage outfits to school and museum outings. After days of viral buzz and online theories, he stepped forward, saying, _“With this photo, there is a mystery—so you have to make it last.”_[Follow his Instagram page here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1y6yAUQOHViM4aLuLnUVC8xp3TpmTAXAkSJDSAzXj3GWcL6c98xfm82ZtLOR3bh9vR9LXYA0fL2DtW2-KjhzIOX9F991jLY4vtcWK1jDJxAbgAtcGl_LKcBLMiMlwIGlCSKcWk-kei8Qwov0shpNaBa-8deO8WFJQvGiQlybw1AKAAlC9q1oGvEhXoIFb0QkycYki91PmrpKNHXPOrPLHO97KTbGLvZ5uW_xO7Tuw6xpjT0brbqtvfxcSue7UnhlowJ9csqSacI-WcmlQTp9vuUv61-p8GPA37CQAA___GU3Bj).

On this day in 1775, the Second Continental Congress established the Continental Marines, the precursor to the , by resolving that “two Battalions of Marines be raised ... for service as landing forces with the fleet.” According to , pecan, pumpkin, and sweet potato pies top the list of Thanksgiving favorites. No surprise there—but it’s never too early to start a spirited dessert debate. What’s the one pie you can’t go without on Thanksgiving? It’s an easy way to take control of your money and plan confidently for the years ahead. | Senate Advances Shutdown Bill The to advance a legislative measure, paving the way to end the 40-day federal government shutdown and potentially reopen the government as early as this week. to advance the measure, while a number of other Democrats, citing the lack of guaranteed Affordable Care Act (ACA) extension within the bill, voted no. The measure secures a future vote on extending expiring ACA subsidies, guarantees back pay for federal workers, and blocks further layoffs. The resulting compromise package for military construction, veterans' affairs, agriculture, and the legislative branch, while funding the remainder of the government through Jan. 30, 2026. BBC Heads Resign Over Doctored Trump Speech The Director-General of the BBC, Tim Davie, and the CEO of BBC News, Deborah Turness, amid a growing scandal over the selective editing of a Donald Trump speech in the BBC's flagship investigative program, Panorama. Davie took "ultimate responsibility" for "mistakes made" at the public broadcaster after a leaked internal memo alleged the program used spliced clips from Trump's Jan. 6, 2021, speech to falsely suggest he called supporters to "fight like hell," omitting his actual call to "peacefully and patriotically make your voices heard." Compare . The whistleblower's memo also revealed that the documentary showed footage of people marching on the Capitol, which was, in fact, recorded an hour before Trump even began his remarks, creating the misleading impression that the march was a direct response to his speech. . Mysterious 'Fedora Man' at Louvre Unmasked The mystery man spotted outside the Louvre after last month’s $102 million jewel heist——has been revealed as a 15-year-old boy. Pedro Elias Garzon Delvaux, a French cosplayer, was visiting the museum with his parents when the viral photo, taken just after the robbery, was snapped. It sparked online buzz as people mistook him for a stylish detective or a possible insider. . In reality, Delvaux says he was unaware of the theft and just happened to be in the right place at the right time, wearing the right outfit. A fan of Sherlock Holmes and Hercule Poirot, Delvaux often wears vintage outfits to school and museum outings. After days of viral buzz and online theories, he stepped forward, saying, “With this photo, there is a mystery—so you have to make it last.”. Honest. Reader-Owned. For two years, The Flyover has grown because readers like you have done more than just open our emails—you’ve believed in what we’re building. Now we’re asking our community to help us take the next step: to make The Flyover truly reader-owned, built and sustained by the very people who read it. We’ve launched a 12-day sprint to reach 2,000 Flyover investors—and as of this morning, we’re just 347 away from that milestone. Each new investor represents more than a number—it’s a Flyover reader stepping forward to say, “Independent, trustworthy news matters—and I want to be part of it.” 3 Reasons to Invest 1\. Keep The Flyover reader owned. From day one, our goal has been simple: the people who read The Flyover should also have the opportunity to own it. This keeps us accountable only to you—not to advertisers, political donors, or outside corporations. Together, we’re proving there’s a different way to build media in America. 2\. Smaller investment, bigger community. We’ve lowered the minimum investment to just $500, making it even easier for more readers to join as co-owners. Whether you invest a little or a lot, you’re helping strengthen a growing network of The Flyover readers who believe in balanced, fact-based reporting. 3\. Protecting honest, fact-driven journalism. Every investment helps keep The Flyover independent and focused on what matters most: clear, fair, and factual storytelling that earns your trust every morning. In a time when so many media outlets are chasing clicks, we’re chasing credibility. Why It Matters When we launched The Flyover , we knew people were hungry for something different. What we didn’t know was how deeply our readers—you—would stand with us. Every new investor isn’t just adding to our numbers—they’re helping shape the future of honest journalism. They’re helping ensure The Flyover can grow into more states, reach more readers, and continue telling the stories that matter most to common-sense Americans. We’re now 347 investors away from reaching 2,000 reader-owners—a milestone that would send a powerful message: that readers, not corporations, can build something lasting, credible, and worthy of trust. According to yesterday's poll question, readers are pretty evenly divided over what to call the evening meal. Check out [this explainer](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFugzAQgOGngQ1054AJg4cubOnaER32ESwdBtkGkrev0lfo_usb_p_H-CAvPjy_aWWTX_M2Br6ScM4cR0frFJnsIlvglClGEnlTcKMC1VaIFcLoyMt7bEpnJnA9lmyw06rrNPS6XEzXa43UTDMwKTtPHTYACqFtJ2vvU19688EQEVAB3lTdNpZpdgC6v893x0UD7HzeYp0XzvyiNMt7OznWdltLMUvOeypuX4UaCjVc11Wn7cgLxyD-9OH5yQo12EPyEblQQzr2nWN1psr5EDiW0bj98iI-6a5o4LmSlz88_2_JadRvAAAA__9v2nds) from _Southern Living_ on the history of the dinner vs. supper debate. Reply to this email to give us the reason behind your take. **Independent. Honest. Reader-Owned.** For two years, _The Flyover_ has grown because readers like you have done more than just open our emails—you’ve believed in what we’re building. Now we’re asking our community to help us take the next step: to make _The Flyover_ truly reader-owned, built and sustained by the very people who read it.

We’ve launched a 12-day sprint to reach 2,000 _Flyover_ investors—and as of this morning, we’re just 347 away from that milestone. Each new investor represents more than a number—it’s a _Flyover_ reader stepping forward to say, _“Independent, trustworthy news matters—and I want to be part of it.”_
* * *
**3 Reasons to Invest** **1\. Keep _The Flyover_ reader owned.**
From day one, our goal has been simple: the people who read _The Flyover_ should also have the opportunity to own it. This keeps us accountable only to you—not to advertisers, political donors, or outside corporations. Together, we’re proving there’s a different way to build media in America. **2\. Smaller investment, bigger community.**
We’ve lowered the minimum investment to just $500, making it even easier for more readers to join as co-owners. Whether you invest a little or a lot, you’re helping strengthen a growing network of _The_ _Flyover_ readers who believe in balanced, fact-based reporting. **3\. Protecting honest, fact-driven journalism.**
Every investment helps keep _The Flyover_ independent and focused on what matters most: clear, fair, and factual storytelling that earns your trust every morning. In a time when so many media outlets are chasing clicks, we’re chasing credibility.
* * *
**Why It Matters** When we launched _The Flyover_ , we knew people were hungry for something different. What we didn’t know was how deeply our readers—you—would stand with us. Every new investor isn’t just adding to our numbers—they’re helping shape the future of honest journalism. They’re helping ensure _The Flyover_ can grow into more states, reach more readers, and continue telling the stories that matter most to common-sense Americans. We’re now 347 investors away from reaching 2,000 reader-owners—a milestone that would send a powerful message: that readers, not corporations, can build something lasting, credible, and worthy of trust.

If you believe in what we’re doing—if _The Flyover_ has become part of your morning routine, your sense of connection, or your hope that real news can make a comeback—[**will you help us reach 2,000 investors?**](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2P2zAMhn-NvCWQZFuyBw0FUm_pLQe0m8CYdKxCH4HMJJd_X7i9At27vg_5gHy_n_0ZQgz5-g0SOf5Yis_03CIxU_UI6VIJ5jWWTBtDrRDjCzJ6LXV_UOqgpEcI8eW7Bt1F4qgacsoaba2Ro2lWp4a5s9bCoi99D2032JHQGOxRms7g0gS3y5RSUmmpWn3su5lgQSnNOCwDkugkYeBSj7wS0wdsS3yVB9XjXFIT3cp820T7RehJ6CnkB218_FlC5pX-mdxxO905-a3c60yiPX3FwKFkoc0eJ8JwT6I9vT0z4eFMGOATzZBuEK5ZtKfpj_EvKJkp8770Pn1mTHW3vP-Y3vz-lu6b6vD2DDGGzVjRyWuCEH9fz__X-cPpXwEAAP__1zGXZQ) Let’s prove, together, that the future of media belongs to the people who read it. ## Dam Breach Prompts Evacuations
Residents of 20 to 30 homes south of the privately owned Carter Lake Dam in Polk County [have evacuated](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuO5CAQAL8GZ4xo8PgROLjE2V56odXQbRstDwuY2fXfn_Z-4eKSSqX687F9oA8-Hb8x8tK-97wl_qqBW-OyEUZbGN0ZcuLasBQM4cZEm1b6KQEkqI3Qh3vrO1qsohk6XmAc9DgOah66c7EAdrLG7NqYkcw42R732Vi0_U701J1ffmQAoEArMPrx7B3jTkoN87RPxKJXTL7l8mgnN_7Guoc7v7k8XI5dWM7WrirML6FXoVe0DswPEXq9cm1Crxy5HJzcLQMjcanyVQ6fDlm4euLUquQ3uhc2lg5L4yIDfrIkjNLleJUcfWWSVw6f0uVXarfMHGXFuwq9wgQG5n4Seu3KQteXD8HXYRS9OiL68C-z_d_c96L_BgAA__9nJpC3) after an 8-inch hole was discovered in the earthen embankment Saturday evening. The discovery prompted the National Weather Service to issue a flash flood watch for the immediate area. The lake's property owners are pumping approximately 14 million gallons of water out of the reservoir to [lower the level by 1 to 2 feet](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u2zAQhE9DdTRIWr8FizTqXtqUwj7t0iK8IgVybT_dPlCukHIwwGC-78_X8gWRY3r8hp28_IS8JPpUJhEqC8L-XQjWjXOiKlAKMJ-QcHHGddpabc2CEPlc2gb9t8HJNuTt0Lth6M3UN5unLlAYaHVudZMbOou2G4c7mg7HCXtoor_GrLXGOmPv7ta1K0FAY_ppDCOSag1hlFxuspHQD9TAZ35Tua15b9hvIkdV91_KzcrNz4D7VSg3XyTKzZxXYOXm-koIp34dCEIaYdcg-ijxfUWGJ-ktM1ZdhQBPHZM-Mj_1ml9JTp2yDpwzxvTQmD-pSiHYm-Lx-ETmWPtBteaxQ-R_v-T_bL69-xsAAP__R7OM3Q), allowing engineers to assess the damage. The process is expected to take up to two days. It's unclear how long the evacuation advisory will [remain in effect](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO2zAMBuCnsTcGkpJY58FDlmxXoFNHg5HoSCglGRJzPr994RR9gm4EPuIH_v_X5_yJkWN-_sBEk3wvZc60NSYRqrPH9KiELnDJ1ARrReYds5-NMlfQGrSaPUbe50vvp4fyo-5p0nYw1g5qHPoweTLk0F_VaEZNaFDZkaw-W6eUNcb2cTrCtNZKG6XP5nS9OMLFKzWMH8uHp-6iyEcp9SSBhL6xLbyXL6onV1LPUxBZW3e-debemfu2bafU8mGduVOGV-vMfX09ODpouJDsgNkDJapPyi7S4YGQJbzh3w9TlYMQPCaIGdbCv8GVV5YdYoNQ2Mf8BAkEqdR83LgIVai0lirk4e94R0aF203_HOxZ93Xy6xaZYxtsd1HPhJHfTeT_9v-azJ8AAAD__3mbnNk). The Texas Commission on Environmental Quality, which issues guidelines for dam safety, is consulting with local officials. Jerry Ash, cousin of one of the evacuated residents, said he [was shocked](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGO5CAQRU-DM1pQNmAHDjZxNptuaJWhGKMuTAsz4_HtV71X2PhLX--9Px_rByZOx-dvzDS3n1jWg66TqTWqa8C8VUK_cznobFgrMt94hBUUGKm11GoNmPhehy7MmwqT7mjWzoJzVk2222erxkkDxAFGN3hvQUdrvXZGx6kfY9-l-X2mtVYalO7hYQZPGINSdhrjGEgMikJqpT7aTo1-8Ix8l2-qD19yx_Pe2usU_S8Bi4Dluq7Hcy9f71HAgrUlzyRgeXsJWLh4ZAGLx9qoSsYnyYBZ-pJfteR0UpAec6BDvgo_pS9fR7sFLDAa6VyASL2RmzFODtQ7uW3bJL3xANYrdMp0dQ6vKzGn0zoxqM-Mif-htv8L_D3D3wAAAP__biyNbw) when he learned about the situation. "_A lot of people out here don't have flood insurance because it doesn't flood out here. It's unheard of_.”
## 25 Years Since Day Without Traffic Death
TxDOT officials and families of those lost in traffic fatalities [gathered at the Texas Capitol](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO5CAQRL8GZz2CHoxNQHCJs730QqsNzRodY1bAjcd_f5r9hY1Lqqr3_nysH5RyOj5_04Ndf8WyHny2zL1zXQM9tsrk91wObp1qpZwvOsKKEkdQCpRcA6V8rXoIbpPBqoGdmgxOk5HWDLvbNilttJ7sPNJkNqJp3DbUfsY5cJyG5N5lSimpUKo73kbtmWKQ0tg5zoGFlhxSL_XWd-78ohbzVZ5cb748huz23r-auP8SuAhczvO8_X3-43cocKHak88scHlzCVxy8ZQFLv0VSgcc4WKqDVo6PEOm1iHQBUeBwNT3BrVQaPA9K3BBY4EsotdGAfs5gsaogLQfIdowzdoYSeo-VBe-zpRzamYSWn4-KOXvv_1nlp8O_wcAAP__s7SP6w) on Thursday to mark 25 years since the last day no one died on Texas roads. More than 91,000 people have died in auto crashes in Texas since November 7, 2000. One of those is [Whitney Presas Wedell](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbHOozAQhJ_GdBvZJjGhoLiG7r_2SrTYS2xlwZG9CeHtT9wrXD2jmfk0f36mH0yctsdvXGmQ75KnjfbKJEJlCrjOhdBHzhtVwVKQ-cAtTFbbGxgDRk8BEx_TtQnDrENvGhpM52zXOd27Jg7UurtFaql3fnatMUHrzt9mvWCPXeeaNJxhxhhtrDatvdyunnAJWrv-vtwDqaumkCSXi0QS-mJd-MgfKhef14aHKPKqqv2l7KjsuO_75VlRTlHZ8aRRduTskZUdzyplR2OUHXWn7Jgg4ocAIWYmSBusB0TCIrBmiVSg0ErrTKXC9i6V4JmYKZzO-q4v8kIBwp7AF6wRsIJ8QxZ4lwdVoC2AZJCCy5I8BEKJ55ymDOG1J-ZUXaeu-rFi4n848n8nfAb7NwAA___cY51X), a nurse and educator killed by a drunk driver in February 2024. "_I have a hole in my heart now that she's not here_ ," said Whitney's mother Monique Presas. "_She didn’t need to leave the way she did_." TxDOT's "End the Streak" [campaign](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D3SpCAUheHVYIYF-NcEBJOY9aQTWhe4KDUoXXCnbXc_1W7h28Bb5zl_nssTYorH-ht2NPQJeTnwrAmJsCwedlsQ3JbygZWgFEjpgsMvSqiBS8mlWDzEdC19440VXssGjZxGNU2j0GOzGdBusL3tH1boyYWg5aQ73zmYrAQrsYnmG5NSCqmE7FQ79A4heCFG_QgPj6wX6CPl0tKGhB-oIV35jaV1eW-S2YhelXW_mJqZms_zbOnjM7VrfjM1g83_iKn5yyo570zNlXKJWJma7xqvVBD-8luKlVOBNTquBn4hlMpz4LeRewTaarvRnppi_OuMKcU6TqwX6w4x3XvoZy--jfofAAD__3E8ifk) challenges Texans to make good decisions behind the wheel, such as driving at safe speeds, getting a sober ride, and avoiding distractions. “ _If we all work together, we can continue to reduce crashes and finally end this streak of daily deaths,_ ” TxDOT Deputy Executive Director Brandye Hendrickson said. "_If we get to just one deathless day, that opens the door for getting another, and another, and another_."
## Tech Student Kicks Big Money Field Goal
Ahead of Texas Tech's 29-7 win over BYU on Saturday, biology student John Machtolff [kicked a field goal](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbGOpDAMhp8mdBk5hiFQUFyTbq-9EoXEQDQhGSWeZeftT8wrbGXp0yfbv_5_X_OXDTGk7a89aOKfNc-JzhqJmcrs7bEUsm6POVFlW4qN8W2TnxHwLpWSCmZvQ3zPXeOnBfyoGpqU7lHrHsa-2SdSre0t6Ba1GrXyd2XXtfUI0OF6h7EJ07VMKQUKQbV4u3eO7OoB-nFYB0-iA_KBc7nxTkw_tq7xnb-p3Fw-mjjtzM8q2j8CjUBznuctvpYlu0dOMSS6LIGmci7vaz5z4SrQuBwjbSTQFPKy2OCpXPz6RqBRSqCBQaD5nJRMbpeVX54SyzWXg4p8BPegIqvLhapcwib9q4S0yadleTi7EtWPdDGXE1NlgWbQqh-0BgAt0DRl8s8zxBhqr0UH22FD_ETj3xXyPeH_AAAA___LEp-4) of his own during the Pat McAfee Kicking Contest on ESPN's _College GameDay_. The weekly contest offers a randomly selected fan the chance to win $500,000 by kicking a 33-yard field goal. McAfee, a former NFL punter and kickoff specialist, has [given away millions](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuu8yAQhJ_G7rCA-BIKir9xl789pbWBxUZawMKbi9_-KHmF08-M5vt-bssNIsW8_oeElt-hLBlfByEz1sVDulcEt1HJeDDUCkQnZL9oqQehlFBy8RDpXPrW27v0RrVo1TTqaRqlGdvNGmkUenCyV2iG0BuUY28G7RAvQRpoo_2MKaWk0lJddDf0DiF4KUdzDVePTS_RRy614w0Z33AEOssTa-dKasluzPvRXP41em70DK_wIMi5PLKLef1kGj1_oBo9f9uC0W2iose0cyxZ7MAiOQiIwpXMeLBwhQhXFCsk9HB2Gydqq_X7KxLFY5yaXq4JIn0_8N_MPa3-DQAA___9iIYO) of his own money to contestants and charities since 2023. Before his kick, Machtolff said he had [missed an extra point](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DHOozAQxfHTQGdkO8SGwsU2dN-2W6LBHoOVwUZ4NiS3XyVX2PpJPz39__zMP5Ao5fU37Oj4Fcuc8aqEzHjOAfblRPAblYyV4TyB6A05zFrqu1BKKDkHSPSe-za4RYZRteiUNdpaI0fTbk5GO44yWOlND6YPxgy29_cYbtEqNSxtch9MKSWVluqmu3vvEWKQ0oxDHAI2vcSQuJwdb8j4ghrpXZ54dr7sLbmN-ajN7Vejp0ZP13V1NX2mRk--EOGKIpbCCxA1evoCgtFvovLfgJkFPDGvWEVlYBR-g_1IJdctHWJPtWIQr0McwGL3EBHFI_lHyqvwJTNWbk8XjisRpWps08t1h0Tfb_x_RZ9O_wsAAP__niaOvQ) as the kicker for Archer City High School when they lost 21-20 to the eventual state champions in 2020. Machtolff's first kick sailed left of the goal posts, but McAfee let him try again, saying, "_This is your last opportunity in maybe your whole life to make a kick_." Opting to kick barefoot, Machtolff nailed his second attempt, winning $250,000 for himself and another $250,000 for a local charity.

For two years, The Flyover has grown because readers like you have done more than just open our emails—you’ve believed in what we’re building. Now we’re asking our community to help us take the next step: to make The Flyover truly reader-owned, built and sustained by the very people who read it. We’ve launched a 12-day sprint to reach 2,000 Flyover investors—and as of this morning, we’re just 347 away from that milestone. Each new investor represents more than a number—it’s a Flyover reader stepping forward to say, “Independent, trustworthy news matters—and I want to be part of it.” 3 Reasons to Invest 1\. Keep The Flyover reader owned. From day one, our goal has been simple: the people who read The Flyover should also have the opportunity to own it. This keeps us accountable only to you—not to advertisers, political donors, or outside corporations. Together, we’re proving there’s a different way to build media in America. 2\. Smaller investment, bigger community. We’ve lowered the minimum investment to just $500, making it even easier for more readers to join as co-owners. Whether you invest a little or a lot, you’re helping strengthen a growing network of The Flyover readers who believe in balanced, fact-based reporting. 3\. Protecting honest, fact-driven journalism. Every investment helps keep The Flyover independent and focused on what matters most: clear, fair, and factual storytelling that earns your trust every morning. In a time when so many media outlets are chasing clicks, we’re chasing credibility. Why It Matters When we launched The Flyover , we knew people were hungry for something different. What we didn’t know was how deeply our readers—you—would stand with us. Every new investor isn’t just adding to our numbers—they’re helping shape the future of honest journalism. They’re helping ensure The Flyover can grow into more states, reach more readers, and continue telling the stories that matter most to common-sense Americans. We’re now 347 investors away from reaching 2,000 reader-owners—a milestone that would send a powerful message: that readers, not corporations, can build something lasting, credible, and worthy of trust. If you believe in what we’re doing—if The Flyover has become part of your morning routine, your sense of connection, or your hope that real news can make a comeback— Let’s prove, together, that the future of media belongs to the people who read it. ** | Dam Breach Prompts Evacuations Residents of 20 to 30 homes south of the privately owned Carter Lake Dam in Polk County after an 8-inch hole was discovered in the earthen embankment Saturday evening. The discovery prompted the National Weather Service to issue a flash flood watch for the immediate area. The lake's property owners are pumping approximately 14 million gallons of water out of the reservoir to , allowing engineers to assess the damage. The process is expected to take up to two days. It's unclear how long the evacuation advisory will . The Texas Commission on Environmental Quality, which issues guidelines for dam safety, is consulting with local officials. Jerry Ash, cousin of one of the evacuated residents, said he when he learned about the situation. "A lot of people out here don't have flood insurance because it doesn't flood out here. It's unheard of.” 25 Years Since Day Without Traffic Death TxDOT officials and families of those lost in traffic fatalities on Thursday to mark 25 years since the last day no one died on Texas roads. More than 91,000 people have died in auto crashes in Texas since November 7, 2000. One of those is , a nurse and educator killed by a drunk driver in February 2024. "I have a hole in my heart now that she's not here ," said Whitney's mother Monique Presas. "She didn’t need to leave the way she did." TxDOT's "End the Streak" challenges Texans to make good decisions behind the wheel, such as driving at safe speeds, getting a sober ride, and avoiding distractions. “ If we all work together, we can continue to reduce crashes and finally end this streak of daily deaths, ” TxDOT Deputy Executive Director Brandye Hendrickson said. "If we get to just one deathless day, that opens the door for getting another, and another, and another." Tech Student Kicks Big Money Field Goal Ahead of Texas Tech's 29-7 win over BYU on Saturday, biology student John Machtolff of his own during the Pat McAfee Kicking Contest on ESPN's College GameDay. The weekly contest offers a randomly selected fan the chance to win $500,000 by kicking a 33-yard field goal. McAfee, a former NFL punter and kickoff specialist, has of his own money to contestants and charities since 2023. Before his kick, Machtolff said he had as the kicker for Archer City High School when they lost 21-20 to the eventual state champions in 2020. Machtolff's first kick sailed left of the goal posts, but McAfee let him try again, saying, "This is your last opportunity in maybe your whole life to make a kick." Opting to kick barefoot, Machtolff nailed his second attempt, winning $250,000 for himself and another $250,000 for a local charity. | Flying together with our sponsor JP Morgan's New $1.5 Trillion Bet JP Morgan didn’t become the world’s biggest bank by investing in the wrong places. That’s why Critical minerals (e.g. lithium) are essential to everything from batteries to the energy transition. Demand is massive and growing. Especially now that producing these minerals has become a matter of national security. Take lithium. By 2040, lithium demand will be 18X the amount currently produced. No wonder giants like JP Morgan are jumping in. It’s also why Their patented technology can produce up to 3X more lithium than traditional methods. Now, they’re preparing for commercial production on a potential $1.1B annual revenue project in Chile, at projected market prices. In a procedural test vote, the Senate approved by a 60-40 margin moving forward [...]

| |
---|---|---

# [Healthcare Costs Soar Under Administrative Burdens](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=178390105&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MzkwMTA1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI3NzY1MDksImV4cCI6MTc2NTM2ODUwOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.kj9yZWsCPsorMguWS9TLXY1wBSaW-E5KFlwF_AAksG4)
### Healthcare administrators that don't treat patients are increasing healthcare costs
| | [Dr. Michael Goldstein](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct) and [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81)
---
| Nov 10
---
| [](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct)| | [](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81)
---|---|---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=178390105&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MzkwMTA1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2Mjc3NjUwOSwiZXhwIjoxNzY1MzY4NTA5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTQ1NDg1MyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.wd6tqYfX0IlBG2QFb-fsFXMXQh1-kc8KhNJjz_2zgOU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=178390105&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MzkwMTA1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI3NzY1MDksImV4cCI6MTc2NTM2ODUwOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.kj9yZWsCPsorMguWS9TLXY1wBSaW-E5KFlwF_AAksG4&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=178390105&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MzkwMTA1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI3NzY1MDksImV4cCI6MTc2NTM2ODUwOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.kj9yZWsCPsorMguWS9TLXY1wBSaW-E5KFlwF_AAksG4)
---
| | [](https://substack..ECh8fr-snJ7recXZbQRVn4oPziElJrLE_2WGwfc4Y6g?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/drmichaelgoldstein4ct/p/healthcare-costs-soar-under-administrative?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

**American Government has become the Administrative State.**
[ Pledge your support](https://substack..npJkX_S6ey4oojRGgZTszQQ3saSPP1olTH1ACoJXj78?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=178390105)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=178390105&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MzkwMTA1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI3NzY1MDksImV4cCI6MTc2NTM2ODUwOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.kj9yZWsCPsorMguWS9TLXY1wBSaW-E5KFlwF_AAksG4)
[Share The Goldstein Substack](https://substack.com/redirect/415e1506-f241-4d96-92d5-fa2616fee119?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The same is true for healthcare - _**As regulation increases here in America, we increase the spend and workforce of healthcare administrators rather than increasing the physician workforce.**_
These healthcare administrators cannot do their jobs so they also have assistants and secretaries and all of this administrative bureaucracy requires office space, computers, phones, company cars, and systems that they can input data and other infrastructure support. Many have nice offices. _**Look at the average size of an exam room, you barely fit an exam table.**_
_**This massive expansion of the healthcare bureaucracy is a direct result of the government transforming healthcare from a healing profession into a quasi government bureaucracy as a de-facto administrative agency.**_
**THE ONLY WAY TO REDUCE HEALTHCARE COSTS IS TO REDUCE AND ELIMINATE ADMINISTRATIVE BLOATING THAT IS RESPONSIBLE FOR 1/3 rd OF OUR TOTAL HEALTHCARE COSTS**.
Every time you and/or your employer writes a check to pay for healthcare premiums, 1/3rd of that is for administrative expenses that are increasing and result in more expensive healthcare. **Administrators have exponentially increased while Physician growth has remained constant.**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/19387b46-38cf-427a-839a-da8cf1d3d360?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
According to the graph and based on my own experience as a physician, it was easier to be a doctor and provide better healthcare in 1980 than it is today.
**Despite better technology, patient outcomes were better and we still had independent physicians** who knew their patients and their families.
_**Now you have a warmer interaction with your butcher at the deli counter as medicine feels like an experience at the DMV.**_
Some critics argue that we need a single-payor model. _**If we ever create a single payor system, the bureaucracy will get even bigger with even less money going to the doctors and other healthcare providers.**_
Despite massive technological improvements, administrative costs should go down and not up. However, that has not occurred in healthcare. _**It seems to defy the logic of efficiency and economies of scale and the only reason for that is poorly written administrative polices due to bureaucracy imposed by our government.**_
We need to Make America Healthy Again by making American Healthcare efficient again.
Medical care should be patient focused where doctors are healers and not COGS in a healthcare factory usually run by a vertically-integrated hospital system that is becoming the largest "private" employer in many states.
| A guest post by| [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81?utm_campaign=guest_post_bio&utm_medium=email)Business Consultant, Political Consultant, Campaign Manager, Construction Consultant and Corporate Attorney. Jonathan served as the Campaign Manager for Dr. Michael Goldstein - CT-04 [Subscribe to Jonathan](https://substack.com/redirect/96790cea-40ba-43aa-a105-bfcab5d804d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

subject: Trump and his allies aren't slowing down

body:
I need your help, Peter
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb38d0c96-f1ef-4f1f-b006-a0264b0ef6ce/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Peter, I'll be honest. When the DCCC added me to their Frontline list earlier this year, I knew this was going to be a tough re-election battle. Mike Johnson and the NRCC have been attacking me since my first vote in Congress. Then, Trump-aligned Super PACs have launched false attack ads in NJ-09, lying about my record. This isn’t the first time dark-money groups have come after me this cycle, and it won’t be the last. This isn't the politics I grew up with, Peter. Our differences got settled face-to-face, through direct conversations with one another. The opposition and I always shared a common goal of doing what's best for New Jersey families; we just disagreed on how to get there. But we settled those disagreements professionally, not with dark money ad campaigns full of misinformation. No matter how things change, Peter, Blue Amp Action and I will respond the way we always have: with grassroots power. Billionaires will never be able to buy this seat. [That's why I'm reaching out to you today. Can you make a contribution of $25 to help me and Blue Amp Action fight back?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1b9b9366-73dc-4f18-8e5c-117bd39e0b39/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade, it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7a9f07d0-b65b-4758-acdc-c06b73cdcbd9/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

body:
This poll changes everything...

Friend,
Ever since the Democrats' government shutdown began on October 1, I placed a pause on our campaign's fundraising.
With the Radical Left holding our government hostage to try and benefit illegal immigrants all at the expense of hardworking Americans like you, honestly, the last thing I wanted to do was bother my supporters with political emails and texts.
In fact, I believe now more than ever, the American people need to see that YOU are our ONLY priority, which is why I've had my own salary withheld during the Democrats' senseless shutdown.
[But today, I have to at least break my silence because I just received information that will UPEND the 2026 elections. ](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zeorpqlr1b5eqt2lcfjntrl04dd2ioledo6bbih8_rp22sh2s8i6eor2ctieao74cph2bs)
[A BRAND NEW POLL in New York just dropped, showing that once voters are aware of our positions, I would BEAT Democrat governor Kathy Hochul by 1 POINT!](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z1rp3hi3nbgurpgu9oiso71v1lb1vnnlv4ue6c8o_rp22sh2s8i6eor2ctieao74cph2bs)
Hochul and the Democrats were desperately hoping that I would continue to pause my messaging during the shutdown so that they could bury this news and prevent us from seizing the momentum.
There's no two ways about it; this poll is devastating for Hochul. She's even being labeled the MOST VULNERABLE Democrat governor in the country.
And this new poll shows us our clear path to victory.
[So long as we reach every last voter, so long as our message isn't drowned out by the Democrats' multimillion-dollar ad buys, and so long as I have YOU in my corner, we can SAVE New York!](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z1jp7rujh1l8uptp7qos2r43s5theejb51erp0e0_rp22sh2s8i6eor2ctieao74cph2bs)
...But all this depends on having the resources necessary to get the job done.
[That is why I've made the calls to secure a 5oo% MATCH for the next 24 hours to ensure we capitalize on this moment.](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zs8mgb62jt0ah01brb51b0q90e198v32gpebcrq0_rp22sh2s8i6eor2ctieao74cph2bs)
Now that our path to save New York is clearer than ever, now that we have all the momentum, will you stand with me in our fight to FIRE Hochul?

body:
Hello, I hope the fall is going well for you.

* * *
# [The Upgrade Cycle](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=178456026&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NDU2MDI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI3Nzk3MjMsImV4cCI6MTc2NTM3MTcyMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.Yv1yxKw9DHNEQ_lCiZP6HGt8zsC1LM7BLFFgmN1HIwo)

---
| Nov 10
---
| [](https://substack.com/@andrewyang)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=178456026&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NDU2MDI2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2Mjc3OTcyMywiZXhwIjoxNzY1MzcxNzIzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjg3MzA0Iiwic3ViIjoicmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.ow9do3rmxlxpnUipCfYW-7Qm5gZd46Ca1sIRP-legcE&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=178456026&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NDU2MDI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI3Nzk3MjMsImV4cCI6MTc2NTM3MTcyMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.Yv1yxKw9DHNEQ_lCiZP6HGt8zsC1LM7BLFFgmN1HIwo&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=178456026&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4NDU2MDI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI3Nzk3MjMsImV4cCI6MTc2NTM3MTcyMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.Yv1yxKw9DHNEQ_lCiZP6HGt8zsC1LM7BLFFgmN1HIwo)
---
| | [](https://substack..rKidnXPZmiQK97GkSDTYXffiZrL_WKvyeUdaIMIKL8s?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/andrewyang/p/the-upgrade-cycle?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Hello, I hope the fall is going well for you.
I have a question: have you bought an iPhone 17?
I haven't, despite seeing the deluge of advertising like everyone else and being able to afford it (not a given). There's a giant Apple billboard near my apartment with a giant iPhone 17 Pro on it in its new gold color.
Why haven't I? I'm perfectly happy with my current device, an iPhone 14 Pro that I got 3 years ago. It's not clear what features the new device offers that would improve my life.
By the way, back in the day, I was a BlackBerry guy, and then a Samsung Sidekick user. Once upon a time, I even had a Palm Pilot. So I've changed devices over the years, but now I don't feel a need.
Chances are this is pretty familiar to you. The average phone replacement cycle has gotten longer and longer for Americans over the past 10 years, going from 2 years and change in 2015 to now approaching every 3 years.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/23f0c86c-9233-4fa2-832f-9c07b0d94ecd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Said the chairman of Verizon recently, "I mean, we remember when we changed the phone every year. That was exciting times. The resilience and the quality of the phones has improved quite dramatically over the last five to 10 years, so people can keep them much longer." Devices aren't innovative to the same degree; the new one is similar to the last one. He noted that the phone replacement cycle is set to exceed once every 3 years for Verizon customers.
When I bought my iPhone 14 3 years ago, I got it 'for free' because I entered a 2-year contract with Verizon to pay it off. But is it free? "The average customer is paying thousands of dollars for that 'free' phone," observes Lauren Benton, the GM of Back Market USA, the leading seller of refurbished devices including iPhones.
She's right in my case; I paid Verizon $140 a month on my new contract that lasted 2 years.
That's $140 x 12 x 2 = $3,360.
If we assume that the baseline cost of my data was about $50 a month - a bit more than what I'm paying [Noble](https://substack.com/redirect/49cda0cb-d4b2-4042-95e2-d9393547d221?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) now - I paid them an extra $2,160 over 2 years, or $3,240 over the past 3 years since I continued to pay them $140 a month even after my phone was paid off.
That's a very expensive 'free' phone.
"The device replacement cycle is how big wireless keeps us stuck in place," Lauren says. "We operate in 18 countries. In only 2 of them are phones even allowed to be 'locked' to one carrier: the United States and Japan. In Europe, it's literally illegal to have a phone locked." Perhaps not coincidentally, the average European is paying about $35 a month for wireless data, less than half the $83 a month that the average American is paying. I've gotten animated about this because that extra $48 a month per person adds up to a tax of tens of billions a year on Americans.
Imagine a country where you simply buy a device at a reasonable price and then connect that device to the network of your choice. That's what most of the world experiences.
"You can buy a refurbished iPhone 15 - which we think is a better device than the 17 - from us for about $500," Lauren says, "and then connect it to whatever network you want." The average Noble Mobile user is paying about $40 a month. The savings to the average consumer are staggering.
I'll admit that, if I hadn't started [Noble](https://substack.com/redirect/49cda0cb-d4b2-4042-95e2-d9393547d221?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), I would have simply walked into the Verizon store to trade in my current phone and get the new model whenever my phone dies; they would have then signed me up for a new 3-year contract (they've made them longer now) and charged me at least the $140 a month I've been paying them for years. I said to Lauren that I've probably given Verizon an extra $15,000 or so over the last 26 years. She said, "Oh, it's a lot more than that given opportunity costs and compound interest. I say to folks that if we can free people of big wireless, we'll literally be funding their retirement."
She's right; Verizon paid $11 billion to shareholders last year in dividends. AT&T another $7 billion. Multiply that by 20+ years? That's enough to make a down payment on retirement for a lot of Americans.
It's why I'm so excited to move us forward with Noble Mobile. Will we stay on a mindless treadmill funding the shareholders of big wireless? Or will we use our newfound freedom from the upgrade cycle to make a better choice that serves us and our values? Your phone is unlocked and you're a free agent. In a country where millions of Americans are looking for ways to save money on our monthly bills, I'm betting on the latter.
For my conversation with Lauren Benton of [Back Market](https://substack.com/redirect/8e78b423-5a7a-42c6-901b-67152c2c56a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about the American wireless industry click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/9d72887a-cedc-4122-a033-ce8f23fbc74d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). [Offline](https://substack.com/redirect/d72ebc7e-3888-42c6-b6a2-1e3fd950c391?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) no-phones party is on the way to Brooklyn, Chicago, SF, and LA this week in connection with the Scott Galloway podcast tour - see you there! Email [matt@noblemobile.com](https://substack.com/redirect/dbd85a6e-dd99-4ef3-a359-666f27a6fbcd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) if you want a few months off from your wireless bill and want to be rewarded with [Noble Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/be42017c-2882-4a41-88a0-13cdd6eb6924?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Look up.

clean_body: Hello, I hope the fall is going well for you. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more | | ---|---|--- * | | | Nov 10 | | | | | | | | | | | | Hello, I hope the fall is going well for you. I have a question: have you bought an iPhone 17? I haven't, despite seeing the deluge of advertising like everyone else and being able to afford it (not a given). There's a giant Apple billboard near my apartment with a giant iPhone 17 Pro on it in its new gold color. Why haven't I? I'm perfectly happy with my current device, an iPhone 14 Pro that I got 3 years ago. It's not clear what features the new device offers that would improve my life. By the way, back in the day, I was a BlackBerry guy, and then a Samsung Sidekick user. Once upon a time, I even had a Palm Pilot. So I've changed devices over the years, but now I don't feel a need. Chances are this is pretty familiar to you. The average phone replacement cycle has gotten longer and longer for Americans over the past 10 years, going from 2 years and change in 2015 to now approaching every 3 years. | | ---|---|--- Said the chairman of Verizon recently, "I mean, we remember when we changed the phone every year. That was exciting times. The resilience and the quality of the phones has improved quite dramatically over the last five to 10 years, so people can keep them much longer." Devices aren't innovative to the same degree; the new one is similar to the last one. He noted that the phone replacement cycle is set to exceed once every 3 years for Verizon customers. When I bought my iPhone 14 3 years ago, I got it 'for free' because I entered a 2-year contract with Verizon to pay it off. But is it free? "The average customer is paying thousands of dollars for that 'free' phone," observes Lauren Benton, the GM of Back Market USA, the leading seller of refurbished devices including iPhones. She's right in my case; I paid Verizon $140 a month on my new contract that lasted 2 years. That's $140 x 12 x 2 = $3,360. If we assume that the baseline cost of my data was about $50 a month - a bit more than what I'm paying now - I paid them an extra $2,160 over 2 years, or $3,240 over the past 3 years since I continued to pay them $140 a month even after my phone was paid off. That's a very expensive 'free' phone. "The device replacement cycle is how big wireless keeps us stuck in place," Lauren says. "We operate in 18 countries. In only 2 of them are phones even allowed to be 'locked' to one carrier: the United States and Japan. In Europe, it's literally illegal to have a phone locked." Perhaps not coincidentally, the average European is paying about $35 a month for wireless data, less than half the $83 a month that the average American is paying. I'll admit that, if I hadn't started , I would have simply walked into the Verizon store to trade in my current phone and get the new model whenever my phone dies; they would have then signed me up for a new 3-year contract (they've made them longer now) and charged me at least the $140 a month I've been paying them for years. I say to folks that if we can free people of big wireless, we'll literally be funding their retirement." She's right; Verizon paid $11 billion to shareholders last year in dividends. AT&T another $7 billion. Multiply that by 20+ years? That's enough to make a down payment on retirement for a lot of Americans. It's why I'm so excited to move us forward with Noble Mobile. Will we stay on a mindless treadmill funding the shareholders of big wireless? Or will we use our newfound freedom from the upgrade cycle to make a better choice that serves us and our values? Your phone is unlocked and you're a free agent. In a country where millions of Americans are looking for ways to save money on our monthly bills, I'm betting on the latter. body:
We blinked. 8 treacherous Senate Democrats defected and moved to vote with MAGA. I am as shocked, disappointed, and enraged as you are.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0011Vl7UJDiDRjr3TzRBNgUxzJsWPGOjaVhWfdGrH9FGcF0BQHqWmvS0UbLsBay8nzFrWYMe3MmFRT5FU1wBP0gyZkpQrCO5aYZ7SuYYdYxhmtSX_lBrzufCBlXE7Jfg_Ay534evTRIaCxM_EkQ6WCkJUXdTqZ2C5nqBVxAOat14BmTPTawcSXQ22TYGvWIL4HzEbtzGioVYtHR6nJxWqRluY65XfUfBGv1&c=225MWCrBMju9Gtd0kwW-w8rheCGjfTjlTQ-K_kIzgYsPze_28tr0Aw==&ch=F3kADlyE5-zNNVYyDfOXpNF9bbQEuB2X-kkzTj1hZIQhajkfnuL64w==)
---
|

Peter,
Dems blinked.
Late last night, after 40 days of the longest Government shutdown in American history, 8 Senate Democrats defected and moved to side with MAGA for nothing.
These are the 8 Senate Democrats who sold us out: John Fetterman, Dick Durbin, Maggie Hassan, Tim Kaine, Angus King, Catherine Cortez Masto, Jackie Rosen, Jeanne Shaheen.
This was a dumb, unstrategic move. We got nothing in return.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0011Vl7UJDiDRjr3TzRBNgUxzJsWPGOjaVhWfdGrH9FGcF0BQHqWmvS0UbLsBay8nzFrWYMe3MmFRT5FU1wBP0gyZkpQrCO5aYZ7SuYYdYxhmtSX_lBrzufCBlXE7Jfg_Ay534evTRIaCxM_EkQ6WCkJUXdTqZ2C5nqBVxAOat14BmTPTawcSXQ22TYGvWIL4HzEbtzGioVYtHR6nJxWqRluR3Gt66nwmAG&c=225MWCrBMju9Gtd0kwW-w8rheCGjfTjlTQ-K_kIzgYsPze_28tr0Aw==&ch=F3kADlyE5-zNNVYyDfOXpNF9bbQEuB2X-kkzTj1hZIQhajkfnuL64w==)
---

All we got to show for working with Senate Republicans is that there would be a vote on Obama care subsidies and that the Senate GOP would (maybe?) *support* them. A pinky promise from some of the least trustworthy people in the world.
This key component of Obama's signature affordable care act will now likely be stripped down and millions of Americans will lose their healthcare.
We just won in a landslide across America on Tuesday. It was a mandate for Democrats to keep fighting. The majority of Americans rightfully blamed Trump and not Democrats for the shutdown. Yet we threw in the towel for no discernable reason.
We are in a primary against a well funded right-wing Democratic opponent who up until just a few months ago was a Republican.
In the aftermath of January 6th they stayed Republican. After Roe was repealed they stayed Republican.
Trisha has been a Democrat her whole life and has the battle scars to prove it. She fought her entire career for working people at the AFL-CIO, where I worked my way up to become their head speech writer. In 2024 when I went head to head with Boebert I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district.
This campaign is about so much more than beating Boebert. It's about electing a Democrat who will fight tooth and nail for us and never back down. I won't, my opponent will.
[](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0011Vl7UJDiDRjr3TzRBNgUxzJsWPGOjaVhWfdGrH9FGcF0BQHqWmvS0UbLsBay8nzFJtY6lTInzFgO7p5NmmeBjgkJ8LI9_FvYiEX7TXoV-2maf_JuvGdDXdbXPKbS9MbqHn9UieC1fuFUfSxl3OvdzPYWe6x6Rdi4VDlUYFBqBHxZWMGbFKjMTeGz1844jDAaXYyjp7Hd0plSw60RFDm2NnBWvhryGRMi8jO0AsyZu_zxozvY95t5sw==&c=225MWCrBMju9Gtd0kwW-w8rheCGjfTjlTQ-K_kIzgYsPze_28tr0Aw==&ch=F3kADlyE5-zNNVYyDfOXpNF9bbQEuB2X-kkzTj1hZIQhajkfnuL64w==)[I need your support now more than ever. Can you make a donation of $50, $25, or even $5, to beat Boebert and ensure that our nominee is a real Democrat and not a Johnny come lately MAGA traitor?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0011Vl7UJDiDRjr3TzRBNgUxzJsWPGOjaVhWfdGrH9FGcF0BQHqWmvS0UbLsBay8nzFJtY6lTInzFgO7p5NmmeBjgkJ8LI9_FvYiEX7TXoV-2maf_JuvGdDXdbXPKbS9MbqHn9UieC1fuFUfSxl3OvdzPYWe6x6Rdi4VDlUYFBqBHxZWMGbFKjMTeGz1844jDAaXYyjp7Hd0plSw60RFDm2NnBWvhryGRMiiuIOO7ntq7cWZ3JBQhB-gg==&c=225MWCrBMju9Gtd0kwW-w8rheCGjfTjlTQ-K_kIzgYsPze_28tr0Aw==&ch=F3kADlyE5-zNNVYyDfOXpNF9bbQEuB2X-kkzTj1hZIQhajkfnuL64w==)

| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001h1SVh91d2DefNNJOSDbk2Cvn--W0EgY6Ze4LMlkP3fRS6Wn4lD5i01DX8-wC7mQVioQVGwOgObx-taqIG-a5dVD0NX8Kn5xhHiKEu4mzeSNOi6OqI-5fuQBWiL2XAub6o9MsCOiI5l4YjL24AXJzsATa3SPys16jdAdffX75vUC5inNxF6MohNLi1bbVSkM6LO_2TiWaHuKzAdKdicV90js-LvvYPBYYfxj4788ppUo=&c=pNeqePmtVCDNFVm3t3eXavzt7YyHHqsc4AbmPmb1kexHEbEF48DfmQ==&ch=0nAgzkUJKxE1J7imzvdLDPcIWS8Xnk9yOwsXFUM3r1BltGu0tp8XPw==)

Peter,
Dems blinked.
Late last night, after 40 days of the longest Government shutdown in American history, 8 Senate Democrats defected and moved to side with MAGA for nothing.
These are the 8 Senate Democrats who sold us out: John Fetterman, Dick Durbin, Maggie Hassan, Tim Kaine, Angus King, Catherine Cortez Masto, Jackie Rosen, Jeanne Shaheen.
This was a dumb, unstrategic move. We got nothing in return.

This key component of Obama's signature affordable care act will now likely be stripped down and millions of Americans will lose their healthcare.
We just won in a landslide across America on Tuesday. It was a mandate for Democrats to keep fighting. The majority of Americans rightfully blamed Trump and not Democrats for the shutdown. Yet we threw in the towel for no discernable reason.
We are in a primary against a well funded right-wing Democratic opponent who up until just a few months ago was a Republican.
In the aftermath of January 6th they stayed Republican. After Roe was repealed they stayed Republican.
Trisha has been a Democrat her whole life and has the battle scars to prove it. She fought her entire career for working people at the AFL-CIO, where I worked my way up to become their head speech writer. In 2024 when I went head to head with Boebert I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district.
This campaign is about so much more than beating Boebert. It's about electing a Democrat who will fight tooth and nail for us and never back down. I won't, my opponent will. [I need your support now more than ever. Can you make a donation of $50, $25, or even $5, to beat Boebert and ensure that our nominee is a real Democrat and not a Johnny come lately MAGA traitor?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001h1SVh91d2DefNNJOSDbk2Cvn--W0EgY6Ze4LMlkP3fRS6Wn4lD5i01DX8-wC7mQV5_y9ZMNeywHqJWSB9kRHDS3lCoZNhM7TZvL-8ZvY-eI4QrnAyCYdhTHszLjB7dWFiSH_WyM4ehX78mAICptEAFzP3cZrkjzL18xWc1J0pGCNMRo9MeGT28uEJthR1oBVNFQHeNy4xrcZOEqyr0J3F53YhLPPwiDrJhgR4hvom-ot7E1GFHWdXg==&c=pNeqePmtVCDNFVm3t3eXavzt7YyHHqsc4AbmPmb1kexHEbEF48DfmQ==&ch=0nAgzkUJKxE1J7imzvdLDPcIWS8Xnk9yOwsXFUM3r1BltGu0tp8XPw==)

# BREAKING NEWS
# KEVIN KIJEWSKI, CANDIDATE FOR MICHIGAN ATTORNEY GENERAL, CELEBRATES PRESIDENT DONALD TRUMP GRANTING PARDON TO MICHIGAN ALTERNATE ELECTORS
---
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=b0e6f9bf79&e=4b10910eed)
| | **Birmingham, MI** – Today, in a resounding affirmation of justice and the rule of law, President Donald Trump issued federal pardons to Clifford Frost Jr. and the other Republican Michigan Alternate Electors who were unjustly targeted in Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel's politically driven prosecution. Atty. Kevin Kijewski (pronounced Key-Ev-Ski), Republican candidate for Michigan Attorney General and counsel for Frost, hailed Donald Trump’s pardons as a powerful rebuke of Nessel's weaponization of the Michigan justice system for partisan purposes.

[A copy of Kijewski’s brief from Dana Nessel’s criminal case that secured the dismissal in September can be viewed by clicking here](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=ef5bd52d39&e=4b10910eed). Kijewski is the only Michigan AG candidate that actively fought against lawfare, both in his words and actions, over the past two and a half years - even when others did not have the courage to speak out and stand up on this important issue.
"President Trump’s pardon further exposes AG Dana Nessel's baseless case and lays bare her long-standing political motivations," said Kijewski. "For years, Nessel has weaponized her office against Republicans, as evidenced by her own words in 2023 when she derisively called the alternate electors 'brainwashed' at a virtual event hosted by liberal activists. [She boasted about shopping the case to a 'very Democratic-leaning county' like Ingham to secure favorable jurors](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=319ca953f2&e=4b10910eed), while admitting these individuals genuinely believed they were acting lawfully and couldn't even plead guilty because they saw no wrongdoing. If that's not an admission of a weak case driven by politics rather than facts, I don't know what is." [You can watch Dana Nessel addressing her inappropriate political motivations to engage in lawfare by clicking here](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=3c46450578&e=4b10910eed).
Kijewski continued, "Nessel's actions were not only inappropriate and defamatory – she undermined her own prosecution by saying the defendants lacked the requisite criminal intent. As I stated at the time, this was a political calculation, not a pursuit of truth or justice. Today's federal pardon from President Trump of my client, Clifford Frost Jr., and the other alternate electors confirms that Nessel's crusade was nothing more than lawfare aimed at silencing political dissent and advancing her Democratic agenda."
The charges stemmed from the 2020 alternate electors who, amid election disputes and following historical precedents like the 1960 Hawaii contest, submitted contingent certificates. Despite Nessel conceding a lack of specific criminal intent, she pressed forward in what Kijewski has long described as a "partisan witch hunt." Earlier efforts, including filing of a [Petition for a Writ of Certiorari with the United States Supreme Court](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=233b025927&e=4b10910eed) in the case of _Frost v. Nessel_ , underscored the overreach, and today's parson further demonstrates Nessel’s case was built on sand.
"Dana Nessel's actions have eroded public trust in our justice system, prioritizing 'messaging' with progressive groups over equal application of the law," Kijewski added. "As Michigan's next Attorney General, I will end these abuses, protect constitutional rights, and focus on real threats to our communities – not vendettas against political opponents. Freedom isn't negotiable, and neither is justice."

In fact, Kijewski fought the MI AG twice - and won both times. Prior to his victory getting Nessel’s baseless lawfare charges dismissed, he fought against a second round of COVID school closures in November 2020 in a federal lawsuit and got the Governor to back down. Kijewski is the only Michigan AG candidate that actively fought against unconstitutional COVID closures - and won.

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | | | | BREAKING NEWS KEVIN KIJEWSKI, CANDIDATE FOR MICHIGAN ATTORNEY GENERAL, CELEBRATES PRESIDENT DONALD TRUMP GRANTING PARDON TO MICHIGAN ALTERNATE ELECTORS | | Birmingham, MI – Today, in a resounding affirmation of justice and the rule of law, President Donald Trump issued federal pardons to Clifford Frost Jr. and the other Republican Michigan Alternate Electors who were unjustly targeted in Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel's politically driven prosecution. Atty. Kevin Kijewski (pronounced Key-Ev-Ski), Republican candidate for Michigan Attorney General and counsel for Frost, hailed Donald Trump’s pardons as a powerful rebuke of Nessel's weaponization of the Michigan justice system for partisan purposes. . Kijewski is the only Michigan AG candidate that actively fought against lawfare, both in his words and actions, over the past two and a half years - even when others did not have the courage to speak out and stand up on this important issue. "President Trump’s pardon further exposes AG Dana Nessel's baseless case and lays bare her long-standing political motivations," said Kijewski. "For years, Nessel has weaponized her office against Republicans, as evidenced by her own words in 2023 when she derisively called the alternate electors 'brainwashed' at a virtual event hosted by liberal activists. , while admitting these individuals genuinely believed they were acting lawfully and couldn't even plead guilty because they saw no wrongdoing. If that's not an admission of a weak case driven by politics rather than facts, I don't know what is." . Kijewski continued, "Nessel's actions were not only inappropriate and defamatory – she undermined her own prosecution by saying the defendants lacked the requisite criminal intent. As I stated at the time, this was a political calculation, not a pursuit of truth or justice. Today's federal pardon from President Trump of my client, Clifford Frost Jr., and the other alternate electors confirms that Nessel's crusade was nothing more than lawfare aimed at silencing political dissent and advancing her Democratic agenda." The charges stemmed from the 2020 alternate electors who, amid election disputes and following historical precedents like the 1960 Hawaii contest, submitted contingent certificates. Despite Nessel conceding a lack of specific criminal intent, she pressed forward in what Kijewski has long described as a "partisan witch hunt." Earlier efforts, including filing of a in the case of Frost v. Nessel , underscored the overreach, and today's parson further demonstrates Nessel’s case was built on sand. "Dana Nessel's actions have eroded public trust in our justice system, prioritizing 'messaging' with progressive groups over equal application of the law," Kijewski added. "As Michigan's next Attorney General, I will end these abuses, protect constitutional rights, and focus on real threats to our communities – not vendettas against political opponents. Freedom isn't negotiable, and neither is justice." In fact, Kijewski fought the MI AG twice - and won both times. Prior to his victory getting Nessel’s baseless lawfare charges dismissed, he fought against a second round of COVID school closures in November 2020 in a federal lawsuit and got the Governor to back down. Kijewski is the only Michigan AG candidate that actively fought against unconstitutional COVID closures - and won. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | I’m committed to keeping you informed and welcome your feedback. As Michigan's next Attorney General, I’ll fight to uphold the rule of law, prosecute dangerous criminals, protect our Second Amendment rights, stop political prosecutions, and defend the hardworking people of this state from government overreach. Very truly yours,Kevin KijewskiCandidate for Michigan AG | | | | | "Follow Kevin on X \(formerly Twitter\") | | | | | "Follow Kevin on Facebook" | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Kevin Kijewski for Michigan, 115 W Allegan St, Ste 900, Lansing, MI 48933 Want to change how you receive these emails?You can or |

This Wednesday is the deadline -- and our baby girl is leading the charge [to make a difference.](https://substack.com/redirect/b6a6ebad-f638-4b81-8d0f-8552534a7a15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
She is an elite athlete -- training six days a week. She takes honors and AP classes and carries a perfect 4.0 GPA. She's disciplined, kind, and focused -- the kind of young woman who believes hard work and heart can change the world.
Click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/b6a6ebad-f638-4b81-8d0f-8552534a7a15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to donate.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b6a6ebad-f638-4b81-8d0f-8552534a7a15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Now she's putting that same drive into something bigger than herself. She's serving as a team leader for her school's **Mini-THON** , a student-led movement that raises funds and awareness for **[Four Diamonds](https://substack.com/redirect/b6a6ebad-f638-4b81-8d0f-8552534a7a15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[,](https://substack.com/redirect/b6a6ebad-f638-4b81-8d0f-8552534a7a15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) an organization devoted to conquering childhood cancer.
These kids train, organize, and fundraise -- not for themselves, but for children fighting the hardest battle of all.
Will you help her reach her goal before the Wednesday deadline?
We couldn't be prouder of her -- not just for her achievements, but for her heart. Thank you for standing with her, and with every family praying for a cure.
**For the kids. For the future. For the fight.**

body:
Corruption is the point
| | | | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01K9Q0JPF0YT3WEM82FBAQ16N8_0)
---
| | | | Hey Peter,**

** Donald Trump told 60 Minutes he “doesn’t know” Changpeng Zhao, **the corrupt crypto billionaire he pardoned last month.** Zhao’s exchange funneled billions into Trump’s family coin deal. **After the pardon, the UAE approved chip sales to a Trump-linked company.** This is not ignorance. It is corruption. We are exposing how Trump’s family keeps profiting while pretending they have no idea what is happening. **Read the facts and share them before this fades from headlines.**

[**Learn More**](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01K9Q0JPF0YT3WEM82FBAQ16N8_1)

– Jesse, DemsGive

Friend, The Trump administration is forcing public servants to prove their loyalty in performance reviews. Employees at the Department of Health and Human Services must show they “clearly and demonstrably support implementation” of the president’s agenda. This isn’t just bureaucratic policy; it’s coercion. Federal workers are being asked to choose between their integrity and their jobs. The independence of our civil service is under attack, along with the rights and protections every American depends on. **At Able Dems, we’re fighting back. We support Democrats with disabilities and our allies who will defend accountability, protect civil rights, and uphold the Constitution.** **[Your contribution powers this movement. Every dollar helps elect principled Democrats who resist coercion and hold power accountable. ### Democrats Need To Look to the Hills, the Hollers, and the Plains before 2026 and Beyond
| | [Matt Royer](https://substack.com/@bytheballot)

| Nov 10
