name: Ben Ray, EMILYs List
subject: Amanda Edwards is headed to a runoff
body:
|
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119026482/581682181/-1958040906?refcode=WUEFHK28_20251110_AmandaSplit_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1057322&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YjMxY2EyZS01OWJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Zj9IeH_VUnKhKcHm_Nroj5NyOIbtXxB-koafPC94XBA=) Peter, I'm writing to you from Houston, Texas, where I'm supporting Amanda Edwards as she advances to a runoff in her election for Texas' 18th Congressional District. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119026483/581682182/-1958040906?refcode=WUEFHK28_20251110_AmandaSplit_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1057322&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YjMxY2EyZS01OWJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Zj9IeH_VUnKhKcHm_Nroj5NyOIbtXxB-koafPC94XBA=) [**Before I tell you more about the state of the race, will you split a donation between Amanda's campaign and EMILYs List to help her get across the finish line?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119026484/581682183/-1958040906?refcode=WUEFHK28_20251110_AmandaSplit_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1057322&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YjMxY2EyZS01OWJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Zj9IeH_VUnKhKcHm_Nroj5NyOIbtXxB-koafPC94XBA=) | [SPLIT A DONATION](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119026485/581682184/-1958040906?refcode=WUEFHK28_20251110_AmandaSplit_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1057322&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YjMxY2EyZS01OWJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Zj9IeH_VUnKhKcHm_Nroj5NyOIbtXxB-koafPC94XBA=)
---
**Republicans have left Texas' 18th Congressional District without a representative for _seven months_.** When Rep. Sylvester Turner died just a few weeks after taking office, Republican Gov. Greg Abbot chose to leave the seat vacant instead of calling a special election because he wanted to help Washington Republicans maintain their slim majority in the U.S. House.

It's crucial we elect Amanda Edwards to represent this district who has gone without a voice for far too long.

Tuesday's election was _close_ — and this runoff will be tough, but we know that Amanda has the grit and determination to successfully cross the finish line.
[**But right now, she needs our help to secure the resources it takes to win. So, please, will you split a donation between Amanda's campaign and EMILYs List to help Democratic pro-choice women take back the House?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119026486/581682185/-1958040906?refcode=WUEFHK28_20251110_AmandaSplit_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1057322&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YjMxY2EyZS01OWJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Zj9IeH_VUnKhKcHm_Nroj5NyOIbtXxB-koafPC94XBA=)

clean_body: | = Peter, I'm writing to you from Houston, Texas, where I'm supporting Amanda Edwards as she advances to a runoff in her election for Texas' 18th Congressional District. = = | = Republicans have left Texas' 18th Congressional District without a representative for seven months. When Rep. Sylvester Turner died just a few weeks after taking office, Republican Gov. Greg Abbot chose to leave the seat vacant instead of calling a special election because he wanted to help Washington Republicans maintain their slim majority in the U.S. House. It's crucial we elect Amanda Edwards to represent this district who has gone without a voice for far too long. Tuesday's election was close — and this runoff will be tough, but we know that Amanda has the grit and determination to successfully cross the finish line. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thanks, Ben Ray (he/him/his) Deputy Chief Campaigns Officer, Federal and Gubernatorial, EMILYs List = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email . We'd hate to see you go, but if you want to stop receiving all email, unsubscribe . Have a question? Box 96612 Washington, DC 20077 United States | ---|---

name: Mark Lamb
subject: Pres. Trump knows I’m a fighter
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARK LAMB FOR CONGRESS
body:
I’m not a politician. I’m a career law enforcement officer and patriot who answers to the people. | [](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=69f1a935c7574acdb2f85074df2c0eb9&_e=t16iFkw313eN0FnoVvViClpgGCNw6eg7CbptnRmV9Uhsw15F4PFoIEw2ix1EewkGLJlZR9_biOXzQBXYqz6nz4DKOVsH53FuVJMACT18uTTf3G7_k3JzrbL1StX7N-SwC1aMWwlPBkgv9PhqnQH_FUZdtTjt11Dex8xKsu-X1OdEKjcjtAx-2wByGsJ0RbXb9x9GMtHvOnnbe9XDBG8XrlDrP8Ims-0RhcmMZo-BT6BAl6qWYed6euZs2aCw6HLSen_08qLmxcFOzCSlzOvwIjQ-TdKZ-KE7c6uf1yE-5JtxzR4EDduGzZv1oG9r2NuLv87PwnAEBCiMErPYP5qL4KTYd-ilSSYAplAQPj7lg7lsISsSRiHkcWGcgIjA-d_vyif6EsWbuUVioYjyg6fiL0XursD2d5HgOHeNE6Md7axcORllHXnamVSRjupZuPA3l4z1JyQsEpCwlKj5rtN_R0Jj8v8U1KQTH4YJ_LMU_BpX5xDvp76VA0AjYv7KPif490bk_CuXqIlrlR3A5pwpb_UbBl_4pCNijp7PJUcaEpzctckoOb4o6WKOIe2RfrMVX7B4V5uaGXxWy98mG9GBTelvV01Jxj82mXxA9HbFoqvaE65U8R2plqrpC28d71lbVi9V-cV1Ec4QyfNhaquC4HDnYuEN1GenxxXn6Cse4BH_YI5fZDW3N-T3mxtkST7x)
---
Fellow Patriot,

President Trump just honored me with his complete and total endorsement for me and my campaign for Congress in Arizona’s 5th District:

[](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=69f1a935c7574acdb2f85074df2c0eb9&_e=t16iFkw313eN0FnoVvViClpgGCNw6eg7CbptnRmV9Uhsw15F4PFoIEw2ix1EewkGLJlZR9_biOXzQBXYqz6nz4DKOVsH53FuVJMACT18uTTf3G7_k3JzrbL1StX7N-SwC1aMWwlPBkgv9PhqnQH_FUZdtTjt11Dex8xKsu-X1OdEKjcjtAx-2wByGsJ0RbXb9x9GMtHvOnnbe9XDBG8XrlDrP8Ims-0RhcmMZo-BT6DUVPFh_VyghZWlKJUaOLJPsWbRrzI1IP_yJ3Zl-bm5it-Mi-tqRob6pzdLqowdB2v-0Azcxb9FJfOcD1RPXCuJ0tdkqvnLKK-8p_H_-2tAyrpuYkIrexmN95MC1-FBoR3mn2sFvA26HtIqcQepggpu_DnuUZ9Ggv2GDpeqban2R8VFhKS1RiRMYzKcOR4w8NMKOukxe4PuwR5tGdGpxX6kmCtsKG-eR7DyorMsEXtgmYlhX7dMAtqumcrHzREvYzpalhlwN19HAmVekuu3ePatzIYEVH8gqNzhvNxQ-XmTvEqLUaOqWQ_2vZlWMuBS53xU2Zg6OpwKLJke-gF3nt-QGDGErQdDkZ6KrerB3GuqCKfQrklcKPFVS5T4VY77KOI0H_QhybgI0cODrqK0gsxIjcEyYOsAFnqchS3XCT45BJsZMQmTxi4aHtL9N4Y01tfeXSDdK8ujITdFfuqenwpJ)

As Sheriff of Pinal County, I stared down cartels, human traffickers, and open-border chaos every single day. I know what it takes to enforce**LAW AND ORDER** , secure our border, and protect our freedoms.

**President Trump knows I’m a fighter—that’s why he put his name on the line for me.**

body:
Someone willing to stand with election deniers and extremists cannot be trusted to serve on our state’s highest court.
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20251110-803&t=1&akid=804%2E294397%2EYHLwdy)
Folks,
**Our extreme opponent, Maria Lazar, is campaigning with one of the loudest far-right election deniers, Michael Flynn.**
Someone willing to stand with election deniers and extremists cannot be trusted to serve on our state’s highest court, especially knowing that our court reviews cases involving voting rights, fair legislative maps, and our democracy itself.
Wisconsinites deserve better.
Judge Chris Taylor has spent her career fighting for fairness, protecting the rights of all people, and upholding the integrity of our courts.
We know Judge Chris Taylor is the best candidate for the job, but this race won’t be won on values alone. **We need your help to make sure Maria Lazar and her far-right allies don’t buy their way to victory.**
[**Can you chip in $10 today to elect Judge Chris Taylor and defeat our extreme opponent?**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251110_1_ak.XuE5vL&amount=10&refcode=em20251110-803&t=3&akid=804%2E294397%2EYHLwdy)

Thanks so much,
Team Taylor

name: Oz, Benghazi 'Secret Soldier'
subject: Open before Veterans Day ends
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shadow Warriors Project
body:
This is Mark “Oz” Geist – United States Marine (Ret.), Benghazi survivor, and still on mission.

| | | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zc9h8plhheo6mql30qnb0saqs3bno66m43mhmc6o_rp22sh2s8i6eor2c3i6co76c1h2bs)
---
|
---
|
---
Friend,
This is Mark “Oz” Geist – United States Marine (Ret.), Benghazi survivor, and _still on mission_.
**This Tuesday, Veterans Day, is the one day we set everything else aside and prove to every man and woman who wrote a blank check to this nation that America still knows how to cash it.**
I learned on a rooftop in Benghazi what it feels like when the country looks away.
That night forged a duty I’ll carry to my grave: _**no veteran, contractor or first responder will ever be forgotten on my watch.**_
That’s why my wife Krystal and I started **Shadow Warriors Project** , and that’s why I’m asking you – today – to help us hit a hard goal:
**750 new donations by midnight on Veterans Day, November 11th.**

|
---
[SUPPORT OUR HEROES](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z5vk28iujufu80p8c52ck7jpi3knf4kumr6vsoo8_rp22sh2s8i6eor2c3i6co76c1h2bs)
|
---
If you want to know what your support will actually fund directly, let me tell you a little bit about what Shadow Warriors Project is already doing:
* Bringing together warriors, particularly those suffering from PTSD, with K9s for training and bonding retreats.
* Writing emergency checks so veterans and their families can cover medical care, groceries, mortgages and more.
* Hosting Christ-centered retreats that reach beyond the veteran to help heal and strengthen his spouse, who is often key to his support system at home.

Tuesday isn’t about a free meal or a hashtag on social media.
**It’s about locking shields – American to American – and showing every veteran that the country they defended still has their six.**
[**Please, help us hit our goal this Veterans Day and give whatever the Lord lays on your heart.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zoecpb8bqonjj6sou3of99rk86vr8jra3fb9anlg_rp22sh2s8i6eor2c3i6co76c1h2bs)
Semper Fi,Mark “Oz” Geist
**P.S.** Midnight Tuesday the clock stops. Be one of the 750 who turn gratitude into action. [Hit the link >>](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2ztmd338o9mdoqtl82tusv2n2i8qn1s8q7uve4l1o_rp22sh2s8i6eor2c3i6co76c1h2bs)

|
---
[DONATE NOW](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zjg1kgj13f96qg0kjek36s59ta640tc4uthbmhv8_rp22sh2s8i6eor2c3i6co76c1h2bs)

subject: The Supreme Court could protect female athletes—will you stand for truth?

Women shouldn’t have to share locker rooms with men.

| | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. **** Dear Defender of Truth, Women shouldn't have to share locker rooms with men. They shouldn't have to compete against male athletes. And they shouldn't be punished for speaking the truth.

But across America, that's exactly what's happening.

Men are being allowed to compete in women's sports--robbing girls of scholarships, medals, titles, and safety.

Now, for the first time in history, the U.S. Supreme Court has agreed to hear two cases--_State of West Virginia v. B.P.J._ and _Little v. Hecox_ --that could decide the future of women's sports nationwide.

This could be a watershed moment in the fight to protect biological reality and fairness.

If you believe women's sports should be reserved for women, we need your voice today.

[Sign the Declaration on Women’s Sports »](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/4sKRuRoRcSzmh7LFJ7FTs_flzOhfWhQG8OHI-qsRSr1Bz_45O6nDbJ78klJOAkrQKTwwGhP7fE4AcO8k3u-NbHBBUYcPYiMsBG6VIzlsHK6Sy8HLYj9G-nCBAZHPlW5h10J8rRxcIWgaslsdngmblNUTCfcOVr1lFK1s7oGJDVpTKeRpgY47qeSoosiJNMnUe3VD2s_U0QAlT2Ek8Y3auUipLIciMNddiSS08v5UozQVfSMMlMwCttGyb__O-3J-1XNaholQGGjfSOlbsXsBTdW-gAkW0b-sB76SaqKS4B3vQnYzdcjpF7PUqVfv79Qgw01NP8b4zjR5s7CEbOMekOcKjJ1UrTU2M_RJwGT6PWgVpBYTIRbSom9zE87NnDEE3hOrZTbBH1-jKJH0SrGY6X2EPS2C2SGjk4t56RIxF9ffmShNUqZlWf0SAwYqj3jx8x6D-n3Ma9nqntTdO6fJbsOxHJhZq6u97w6thpu6v5nZDtkxz9N3MM6LmTOez85Rn0jvcezQdCL5shfSw2Q2KhKcKcUPf4NFAzugkoHRQKIFlVFC0BtQ0XPSB89PRHVFw3kBLfqubNb8_mQueseYaR12HtLubCimZA-uiOBPpGZ-o7nF-QNx337uPgDeZoLxX-bH81_xuUsakhPnxIEGu49FAdXRzm2TJZGfCl5rix2tbzuMVlb77sIarbb2nQJsHBdl0invqbX12MkH0BlT7X3ZHjo4QnjuKIIyfSkZedm0ktKolwbeDJ_-DjXz/JAyQgroIe7-EfikgQ5SeqmbqjA5PJXwX/14)

Your signature will say clearly:
✅ Men should not be allowed to compete on women's teams
✅ Women deserve privacy, fairness, and safety
✅ Radical gender ideology must not ignore biological truth

After you sign, you'll be taken to the next critical step...

Make a gift to support female athletes like Lainey Armistead, a courageous former college soccer player who is helping defend her state's Save Women's Sports Act in court.

Your gift of any amount will help Alliance Defending Freedom stand strong at the Supreme Court--at no cost to our clients.

🚨 We've won 16 cases at the Supreme Court since 2011. With your help, and God's blessing, we can win again.

Please add your name now--and then take the next bold step to protect women's sports with a generous gift.

[Add your name and make your gift »](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/Q0EBf9oisbqjj9MqJNwdqLnSKNKaAknAW-BsAj10kapa9ADTma4poHh98oCncWYvMH0KwNEAb4BbeRT-PF_EthG6YHaVLPJCvrgu--yAVmEd5R57iKOS9_6CZV6GjQ3LCWhwtHDA4CMP4JgVqcMLuXp1AYZy0CgvCh7liqLInT8kyIiRfIaGOW8UrSJFcZ5zxoi6UAj1AGrdF8e2mDAW4eYb1PrmXFIFX7DOzZz1XcoMEGoFLRAVir-AP4thv0hE-8qfZTSvexQlmHvDrHYXaK7HcjxLuPjd2MpwDob7XxLpsSa-i_USZKDb_5wIQKapZxzNYfegc6qRwliKPGN7MwSYmFWR3RKd6FcimJPcYtEFSgiuil24uDEy2hh6iM-41tXPxPpQsZdFMNJ4k7O351VJ8l83_RCq3V2TDMYLzA2SS70a3TkfapOL2l_Bj6hApqMWGegSCYZOGGZ0kYPQYOrCseKTE8D7nqbZULtwsBFWH97iVxhT_Li9M5TcW8pqDzb5zRkjeHsE85tLAQNfyurqo-gKglrSEX4O1PbfnqW5tvHrhtmWxnq6NVRkX6nj9FrI55JcYILyOgzwyG_Rf_ISRHi49dmhh-3O9H1FoxzfvQtvH3cjn7gNaXKBWS04al5q5cOgi-enISdK0geujfd7lQY0qoPqtHnhfFkdHYUCZ9KJ8Sq_WiuwiY3sOoWUHN6sfbDAZXP9UEj6_WEX404N7aeanFeoZP1eJzAmiuzu2SZSJjzY0z9IfgNw/viaf11Wfx7HZsYW3Yo0F8OGrSf7RYGcj/14)

Together, we can ensure every girl has a fair opportunity to play--and win--on a level playing field.

Thank you for standing for truth. With gratitude, **Suzanne** **Beecher**
Legal Counsel [](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/_R-8jimZ4YvzfIkcPlvLu7oUeDYffWfNy3VaX6nUj23xOIpJZi7Vv4s-QDtWnxTkiRwKl0rRHJWu7xk9LO2qvvb3xbKDOWnUtWV0kB9UIKN75KQHQqipilROX--Jt6cuamU7-cuZ1EEgVTvGAx3Q0cUvLi40aW6AxsqQPxlI7cuULP4XPAh7X3IPD2Btub9UQyZvhCDl81_qqa0uP_10dCY9m7zVKZs4qY-slj-VDmlXJfm08zLuEse1mPsWg0YicZ-2bbxBCf7NlH-W7gzf68LH3BhorhgBlnmd1KSIjdDjpMcgmYoDBE9amj1MzGach-wc3CARJoBfdc0SKin96X4dth4XW0YU7PifJ8Ol420_lj0dxS9z5PkSMA33RZZ2MPzDAKKr7u-MlU4Kif9WR5GjzOUp-BNmq87av3cCySLe5eadHbT4Ri01l9B_HcITUli77CJs_c2lNOt4rtcRR0uWltETl5F147xz2l-XypTEzYBt8jYRrlh3Pa-FlIzWnmGB5t5NcX5u_0MzqlYdHYHmA_FaW57F2nvMpwIWAP7jdMfm2OwFN9bIh-H5zT6A2aAhEcvRoG8meRoJcuRrYiQCAnV743WFigqVHi8nT78y2f1JPHBSU3wh_5tnUVBwT3zjlOI3bBYN1Dz_uYBKKLN6cWGvCGV7oAyY0UHHPGp1tAhAH80K-jFzLeP-KeRNq6HxfDCjicpIhDO4sVY-B67a3M3VVIzhV0nrciMyzjfikYY0aqgGO3P4BwD9/58F1bZEkJ9_hIjtvi_RM-RpA_E2DlWIt/14)

name: Abdul El-Sayed
subject: Senate Democrats just caved
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abdul for U
body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1261%2E251025%2E_YX8GA ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1261%2E251025%2E_YX8GA ]Abdul for US Senate

Peter,

ICYMI: Multiple Senate Democrats just gave in to MAGA Republicans to end
the government shutdown — and they got nothing in return to protect
healthcare for 25 million Americans.

The only thing Senate Democrats received was a “promise” from MAGA
Republicans to hold a vote on healthcare in the future.

A promise is only as good as the trust you have in the folks making it, or
the leverage you have if they break it. And now, those Senate Democrats
just gave up their leverage to MAGA politicians we know we can’t trust.

Peter, this is
insane. We should be absolutely furious because this is a betrayal.

But at the same time, I can’t help but think, this whole fight — about
shutdowns, subsidies, and who “deserves” care — wouldn’t even exist if we
had Medicare for All.

Every American should have a baseline public health insurance plan that
covers comprehensive care, including hearing, dental, and vision. That’s
what Medicare for All can do.

We already spend enough money in the system to make Medicare for All a
reality. But right now, that money is being siphoned off by corporate
executives in the insurance industry who profit from keeping healthcare
incomplete.

That’s why we need leaders with the moral clarity to call out this
corruption and fix it, not the old-guard politicians who take money from
corporate lobbyists and protect the broken system.

I’m the only candidate in this race fighting for Medicare for All, and the
only one who’s never taken a dime from corporate PACs.

But I can’t win this fight alone. The same health insurance lobbyists who
helped break our system are already spending big to boost my
corporate-backed primary opponent.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251110-1-ak.aevMfB&amount=10&refcode=em20251110-1259&t=1004&akid=1261%2E251025%2E_YX8GA ]Can you pitch in $10 to help us take this fight to
Washington and make healthcare a human right for every single person in
this country?

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251110-1-ak.aevMfB&amount=10&refcode=em20251110-1259&t=1023&akid=1261%2E251025%2E_YX8GA ]Donate $10 ››

Thank you,

Abdul

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. To contribute via check, please address it to Abdul for U.S. Senate, PO Box 126, St. Clair Shores, MI 48080.

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.abdulformichigan.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=1024&akid=1261%2E251025%2E_YX8GA
Update Your Information: https://act.abdulformichigan.com/signup/update-your-information
Unsubscribe: https://act.abdulformichigan.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1025&akid=1261%2E251025%2E_YX8GA

Email us: info@abdulformichigan.com

[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e1304606/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9ob3VzZS1nb3AtYmF0dGxlZ3JvdW5kLWZ1bmQvaGdiZl9kb25fb21fZXJfbGEtbnlnX3JzXzIwMjUxMTA3Lw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMTA/LbmNsckdCQ2VBbw/qP3JlY3VycmluZz10cnVlJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImdXRtX3Rlcm09MjE4NThfd3Iy/gaRIkAQ/s6jd7356eca)

clean_body: | | | | Peter, I’m in. I’ve just announced my campaign for Governor of New York. The tide is turning in the Empire State and the numbers show that voters are ready for a change: That’s right.Recent polling shows me LEADING the worst Governor in America Kathy Hochul, and I am just getting started. Peter, the stakes couldn’t be higher... AOC, Zohran Mamdani, and Kathy Hochul are going to destroy New York . Peter, that’s where you come in. I am humbly asking for your Day One Endorsement. I have a reputation in D.C. for winning hard races, from flipping a deep blue Obama district Republican in my very first campaign, to ousting Liz Cheney from GOP Leadership, I have never backed down from the tough fights that truly matter. So, I’m asking you to stand with me for one more tough fight. . In Liberty, Elise Stefanik Proud New Yorker | | | | | | | | | | %unsubscribeurl% |

name: Building Blue
subject: Here's how we win.
body:
We Build Blue. | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.jTd4CJP2oHMhBHdrjS9uuj9lON4kJdoHrzrshzPmhg6E7kL1xBAi-DQRzVdOLqlANHgdcVBRAXjB0BTeTNviiGKuSCazl6_kMykJzyT2ZDvYDlxRrQBRi5KbFdxCpWEtwTAUN8iGiMlVa4xirdN5m-pcGSw673fHeGak8bs6wP5p1M91215TZe7FWNPwGztd8zpsF350uBLYS0n6NQNEtvcxRlE2uxNgx_64-qFyuog/4lh/F7xuzfriTG-0QnScMO6-uQ/h0/h001.FEeNXHGA9oMsiuA_AQjubMIrCrJnHzqkYIJBwgQWKo0) | | Peter,

We’re glad you’re here. **_[Building Blue](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMa0Pr2UZFNtZKz-B_eZ_H8i1p_3EzsfrfosCjUlWlIojffLtXFv3DKUzY8JzJkcRPPzXP8XCj0XJTHwq42laFtYmLETkf34yRKwAsIWaqJMLPfpVUC6j6txKvwS8cIroHrsQp_GHfkU3IAIb7Jp99SWPnU0Ms04QFaXBLO5Zt3UtEX4qF3VB437AKvU5HAzQdL20HBBjGY-FOmoY2BJczHbFuOk2KzQ-736owRzvH6AQ/4lh/F7xuzfriTG-0QnScMO6-uQ/h1/h001.kMjWdnSsp5N_IShnw2FY-UmtE8VV_8FhJmcwhICDoto)_** exists for one reason: to win elections that protect our rights and strengthen our democracy.

This isn’t just about the next big presidential race — it’s about every seat, at every level. _In 2026 alone_ , **435 House seats, 33 Senate seats, and more than 1,000 local races** will be on the line. Every single one matters.

Our strategy is simple but powerful:

* **Build Power:** Support candidates who can win — from local school boards to Congress.
* **Fund the Fight:** Level the playing field against dark money and corporate interests.
* **Elect Democrats:** Mobilize voters, organize communities, and protect our freedoms.

MAGA extremists are counting on us to sit out. We won’t. And now that you’re with us, neither will you.

Let’s get to work.

– The Building Blue Team

name: Tim Walz
subject: Health insurance premiums will skyrocket
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
40 days later, we are no closer to relief for millions of working families.
#
Peter, I wanted to make sure you heard it from me after this news broke.
Eight Senate Democrats just voted to advance a funding deal that would reopen the federal government and fully fund SNAP for the rest of the year, but let Affordable Care Act subsidies expire.
**Let me be clear about what this means. It’s exactly what Republicans wanted all along. And if these subsidies aren’t renewed, health insurance premiums will skyrocket for millions of working families who are already stretched thin.**
After the longest shutdown in our country’s history, folks are still not getting the relief they need. Families can’t wait. They can’t afford another hike in their health care bills, and they shouldn’t have to.
I’m frustrated and disappointed. Democrats cannot let the Trump Administration steamroll Congress into cutting taxes for billionaires at the expense of working people. That’s not how we do things in Minnesota, and it’s not how we should do things anywhere.
**[I’m going to do everything in my power to continue standing up to this cruel administration while lowering the costs of living for Minnesotans, but I can’t do it without you. Make a grassroots contribution today to fuel our ongoing fight.](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251110_1.3FGvIy&amount=5&refcode=em20251110-60&t=1&akid=60%2E34582%2Eat2UIh)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251110_1.3FGvIy&amount=5&refcode=20251110-60&t=20&akid=60%2E34582%2Eat2UIh)**
With gratitude,
Tim

Under Governor Walz’s leadership, Minnesota is showing the world what’s possible when we come together with grit, compassion, and a shared sense of purpose.
We secured free school meals for every student, cut child poverty, passed Paid Family Leave, delivered record investments in education, and more.
**[Now we need your help to stop MAGA Republicans from dragging us backward. Pitch in $5 today to help us win this race and keep moving forward — together.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_tw_footer?refcode=em20251110-60&t=21&refcode2=60_34582_at2UIh&akid=60%2E34582%2Eat2UIh)**
**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_tw_footer?refcode=em20251110-60&t=22&refcode2=60_34582_at2UIh&akid=60%2E34582%2Eat2UIh)**
Email us: [info@walzforgovernor.org](mailto:info@walzforgovernor.org)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.walzforgovernor.org/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.walzforgovernor.org/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=23&akid=60%2E34582%2Eat2UIh)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.walzforgovernor.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=60%2E34582%2Eat2UIh)

Prepared and paid for by Walz for Governor
Tim Walz for Governor
P.O. Box 4337
St. Paul MN 55104

My opponent has been gaining on me in the polls. Far-right actors are attacking me online. National GOP groups are pouring money into NC to defeat me and our people-first campaign. _This is all weighing on my mind, Peter._ I know we have a tough race ahead of us — the most competitive of my career. The coming months won’t be easy. **But here’s what keeps me going:** We’ve earned financial support from tens of thousands of folks across the country. More than 90% of donors are giving $100 or less. Even more are sharing news of our campaign online. Together, we’re building the momentum we need to win one of the most competitive U.S. Senate races in the nation. I’m deeply grateful to everyone who has joined us so far. [I’m asking for you to join our fight by splitting a contribution between our campaign and The Democratic Party of Georgia today. Would just $5 fit in your budget right away?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119028612/581689972/1816079160?amount=5&source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_111025_COOPERSPLIT_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiOGY4MDkzOC01ZmJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rPkqfLVm_0LsCpDD1YJ8EzAzR1rjwK9ex6ruCvKxqDA=) | | **[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119028617/581689974/1816079160?amount=5&source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_111025_COOPERSPLIT_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiOGY4MDkzOC01ZmJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rPkqfLVm_0LsCpDD1YJ8EzAzR1rjwK9ex6ruCvKxqDA=)**

I appreciate your strong support.
Roy Cooper

clean_body: The voters have spoken. Mike Johnson is ignoring them. | “Frustrated Arizonans have waited more than a month for their new congresswoman to be seated” \- CNN On Election Day, voters sent a clear message, Peter: They've had enough of Trump. Democrats swept Virginia, New Jersey, and New York, and won a critical redistricting fight in California. But voters spoke WEEKS AGO in Arizona, and Mike Johnson is still ignoring them. On September 23, Arizona voters elected Democrat Adelita Grijalva to represent the seventh district — 48 days ago. Johnson claims he's waiting until the House reconvenes to swear in Grijalva, but he swore in two Republicans this year when Congress was out of session. The real reason? Grijalva would be the 218th signature on a bipartisan petition to release the Epstein files — the exact vote needed to force it to the House floor. Johnson is blocking her from taking office to protect Trump. Meanwhile, Arizona voters are being robbed of representation. Grijalva can't hire staff, can't open district offices, and can't respond to constituent concerns. Onward, Unite the Country | Unite the Country is dedicated to electing Democrats who support common sense policies, champion middle class values and are willing to engage Americans regardless of where they live — but we can’t do it without you! At this critical moment in American history, we’re calling on you to join Unite the Country in this fight. = = = Follow along:** | Paid for by Unite the Country, , and not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. name: John Padora
subject: Another “deal” for DC - another hit for working people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Padora for Colorado
body:
| [|
---](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/AnZJfE9IkGOsH9_uvKdYVA~~/AAUS1hA~/7hoUud3P0PU0UqxQHMkjfTxa6qeW2wjnW0WYbSP5tY_napXmInREgrlp4ls6-Y83LCw-Qj-xTAzpC9xiR6FAwwMMuLH3hyp5sgQo0-5ctuyQA_1NNoFRD6S8gd-D3oyu)
| | **Friend,** **After weeks of a brutal government shutdown, eight Senate Democrats just voted to reopen the government -**without a single real win for working families.** ** No protection for Affordable Care Act subsidies.
No relief for the millions struggling with rising food, housing, and medical costs. Just another short-term deal that punts our problems past the holidays. **This isn’t governing - it’s surrender.** Every time the pressure’s on, the Democratic establishment bends to the same corporate interests they claim to fight against. And while they cave, the cost of living explodes, small towns crumble, and working Americans lose faith that Washington gives a damn. I’m running because I refuse to be part of that cycle. I’m not a party insider or a career politician - I’m a working dad and engineer who’s lived through what these “temporary deals” actually mean. Missed paychecks. Skyrocketing bills. Broken promises. **Cuts to SNAP that leave kids hungry. Families choosing between rent and medicine. Health insurance premiums doubling overnight for the people who depend on it most.** We don’t need more politicians who negotiate with fear — we need **fighters** who lead with conviction. **Folks, if you’re as tired as I am of watching Democrats fold while Republicans gut the country, I’m asking you to stand with me today.** Chip in now to build a movement that won’t cave, won’t sell out, and won’t stop fighting for affordable healthcare, good-paying jobs, and a government that actually works for us. [**Working families deserve leaders who fight for them — not cave when it counts. name: Roy Cooper
subject: Something’s weighing on my mind, Peter
body:
Your support keeps me going.
My opponent has been gaining on me in the polls. Far-right actors are attacking me online. National GOP groups are pouring money into NC to defeat me and our people-first campaign.
_This is all weighing on my mind, Peter._
I know we have a tough race ahead of us — the most competitive of my career. The coming months won’t be easy.
**But here’s what keeps me going:** We’ve earned financial support from tens of thousands of folks across the country. More than 90% of donors are giving $100 or less. Even more are sharing news of our campaign online.
Together, we’re building the momentum we need to win the most competitive U.S. Senate race in the nation. I’m deeply grateful to everyone who has joined us so far.
**[I’m asking for you to join our fight by splitting a contribution between our campaign and Wes Moore today. Would just $5 fit in your budget right away?](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251110-1.6Bodlc&refcode=em-20251110-1&t=3&akid=901%2E1086043%2Ed2HjHl)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251110-1.6Bodlc&amount=5&refcode=em-20251110-1&t=22&akid=901%2E1086043%2Ed2HjHl)**
I appreciate your strong support.
Roy Cooper
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251110-1.6Bodlc&refcode=em-20251110-1&t=23&akid=901%2E1086043%2Ed2HjHl)

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20251110-1&t=24&refcode2=901_1086043_d2HjHl&akid=901%2E1086043%2Ed2HjHl)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=25&akid=901%2E1086043%2Ed2HjHl)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=901%2E1086043%2Ed2HjHl)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

name: Team Bell
subject: We get the last word
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
Missouri Republicans think nobody's paying attention while they gerrymander our state, but folks across Missouri know exactly what's going on. Peter,

The Missouri Legislature just did Donald Trump's bidding and pushed through a congressional map designed to protect themselves and their party, not the people they're elected to serve. They think nobody's paying attention while they gerrymander our state, _but folks across Missouri know exactly what's going on._

**Missouri voters are going to get the last word, and we plan to reject the GOP's maps at the ballot box next year.**

We're putting the power back where it belongs: In the hands of the people. Right now, volunteers across the state are collecting signatures to overturn the GOP's rigged map. **[If you're with us in this fight for fair maps and fair representation, add your name next to Wesley's today →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119029692/581693480/-1441531629?ms=em25_251107_redist_adv_gq_act___tb_ettx&refcode=em25_251107_redist_adv_gq_act___tb_ettx&source=em25_251107_redist_adv_gq_act___tb_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251107_redist_adv_gq_act___tb_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=EKFAq5VPR2rQRUm2xwrbv7rYIJbduKQGFGh5QTkOIRsc4hwcCrg+SXbOK4LcrXdfpXbZV0MD6uT59i4RcVkoVsMRMg0zYXt12VbHogNjXOI8nBgWQ85qQl5lzZzlSwbBHjBhmXaPX5V8ToX1MdNmz1BVc8Kfzrx26Za4hV/oTsU=&refcode2=23385277-60be-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjMzODUyNzctNjBiZS1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAyMjQ4MjNmZjliIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=RTX_gLa-ogmSFma-0GS8VrJt4QJlc0bjncnBiZW8KHs=&emci=10ff4893-f9bb-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=23385277-60be-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=1020964)**

Peter, Republicans' goal couldn't be clearer: Follow Texas's lead, slice up our communities, and gerrymander Missouri so Democrats don't have a fighting chance to take back control of the House.

That's how desperate Trump is to cling to power, keep extremists in charge, and expand his razor-thin MAGA majority in Congress. On top of that, this mid-decade gerrymandering will break up communities of color, weaken the votes of already underrepresented Americans, and keep Black and brown Missourians from having fair representation in Congress.**We have to fight back.**

clean_body: Missouri Republicans think nobody's paying attention while they gerrymander our state, but folks across Missouri know exactly what's going on. Peter, The Missouri Legislature just did Donald Trump's bidding and pushed through a congressional map designed to protect themselves and their party, not the people they're elected to serve. They think nobody's paying attention while they gerrymander our state, but folks across Missouri know exactly what's going on. Missouri voters are going to get the last word, and we plan to reject the GOP's maps at the ballot box next year. We're putting the power back where it belongs: In the hands of the people. Right now, volunteers across the state are collecting signatures to overturn the GOP's rigged map. Peter, Republicans' goal couldn't be clearer: Follow Texas's lead, slice up our communities, and gerrymander Missouri so Democrats don't have a fighting chance to take back control of the House. That's how desperate Trump is to cling to power, keep extremists in charge, and expand his razor-thin MAGA majority in Congress. On top of that, this mid-decade gerrymandering will break up communities of color, weaken the votes of already underrepresented Americans, and keep Black and brown Missourians from having fair representation in Congress.We have to fight back. Thank you, Team Bell | = = PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI PO Box 190669, ST LOUIS, MO 63119

## Commonsense Corner:
---
After dragging hardworking Americans through the longest shutdown in history, a few smart Democrats finally did the right thing. [Eight](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/2vbp524Yvvc8UMYnx_AXXna_MX2F6cSQAWwq1pSU4npmNUCJkFKna6JC0Eht9SibTVrov82amTfQamtAVSOqDFBh7spTEALZX4yLlhmxW3TlXWveQa0E8L4lda1C4SKPBtGbKrnuSAS3mP5e8SGOQcOu7J7FynAvt5nfhYNxMnh0nzGZ_sLoBtzPGzMs6ioUPVAcN1gGa-4iV9qkTGMq5Y_m5125y6JdpKGARYLO_9PHymiQUus-C73LhXl8qPDI6h1svaSjm5mcTFphaJodxGLkUaDcRUkbFJ2nJy-bAzejEcgSKGSd6LX6lqEtZk3vJsK1J911xJUaZ-aDTgLYWCvpoPC6gBTtYXqEqpQqcw6uScmE0IrGoHKRXvh6JIjOxqMyjdu9JRcBhSuouWbn55_K6Tij3t-PaelZ2VeIxxvfO7Jcd-VDqD8Pdx1a9XK9syxiL2d0p8EvNkrDmHA5ryBqVnYOyRFWwBLI0K_RJeLcgXuSDIXYq5vS4mw79BeQuTe99lruU8nCIQUgn0EOugqpF9A3j2QG63uZ/APYH096-du390WObAfF6RBsYDWdO-E0q/14) of them broke ranks Sunday night to help the Senate pass a short-term spending deal that reopens the government through January 30, 2026. That means critical agencies like the [VA](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/wO7clpTYwS506y7v7rREcJcRRtGGeMQ6rTfNnw6DWojwkpWKD-ByPucNOt8Ib9Lw4vt5o4STQ9VMxZTxqB4fdzKGAK_vBnet9flIt5RjkMOuvq6Co2omDhooh64MKBOnfkTjCPQuBX-K7FsHK-3W2ff0mIGyh0xP-ehv7_kiieB4UqRJ9A6KcN9hA2cZF_PU42Ar7ZnIv6lXd5mhVen2CCG8NP5B8K3Axl-Hph4mbr1PnDLFAnl5uw6mnSVNBVvIf0lz54BuuLI4IPcixcNmR8CbJfd3E5t3Di99Rso9Tuvc7Xede-OVd1L00FVD-ydNrgKItDDm2-Ioenpn7uZo6x9GHRmYxBE7m0ABl6vYNaZFTmn-zIOumvx2T6TpdmtEPnkI3zkGOEEFr7wYQLBPqxlUXS-0rFJIyBFyxS1a5M_hUfoGGyorQdWaXiDgaW9RhgKh1illqw8Lp3q3P6uJ/rL9snTo7JMGwYoHKkHpPfzEDm7xpsQ1h/14) will get funded, SNAP benefits will go out, and federal workers will get back pay. Meanwhile, Democrats got a promise for a future vote on their Obamacare subsidies—but we all know how that’ll go. _Insert eye roll here._ **Let’s be honest: This shutdown was never about health care or helping families. It was about the Left’s pursuit of power and control.** While Democrats played politics, Republicans stood strong. We didn’t give any concessions on the ridiculous Obamacare tax credits they were holding out for because we’re delivering on the mandate we received from Americans last year. Bottom line: Another stunt from the radical Left just went down in flames.

That means critical agencies like the will get funded, SNAP benefits will go out, and federal workers will get back pay. Meanwhile, Democrats got a promise for a future vote on their Obamacare subsidies—but we all know how that’ll go. Insert eye roll here. Let’s be honest: This shutdown was never about health care or helping families. It was about the Left’s pursuit of power and control. While Democrats played politics, Republicans stood strong. We didn’t give any concessions on the ridiculous Obamacare tax credits they were holding out for because we’re delivering on the mandate we received from Americans last year. Bottom line: Another stunt from the radical Left just went down in flames. | Boots on the Ground Poll: Who’s really to blame for the record-breaking government shutdown—Democrats playing politics or Republicans standing their ground? | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Don’t let your savings be next. | JD Vance says protect yourself. | | | 🟡 News From "American Alternative Assets" | (Sponsored) ---|--- Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. We’re now in the second month of Republicans’ government shutdown – the longest in our nation’s history. And while millions of Americans face anxiety and uncertainty about receiving SNAP benefits to keep food on the table, the Trump administration is actively fighting to freeze this critical support. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119030296/581696021/593712779?source=SBD_VF_EM_PET_111025_SNAP_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NjNkOTk4Yy02M2JlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=29sBR0XXWUKGqIE_fo2L_QZM9br41vxgw_ld6ubJc8U=&emci=4eccb088-8fba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=763d998c-63be-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=799895) Millions of people across the country — including thousands right here at home — are suddenly without the help they rely on to feed their families. Parents are wondering how they’ll put dinner on the table. Kids are showing up to school hungry. **It’s shameful. It’s cruel. And it’s completely avoidable.** Because here’s the hard truth: none of this has to happen. Donald Trump and Congressional Republicans could act today to fund SNAP, reopen the government, and protect families and children, but they’ve chosen not to. Every day that this shutdown continues, the consequences grow worse. Communities are being forced to stretch already limited resources, local food banks are under strain, and families are facing impossible choices just to get by. **[Will you add your name today to demand that Donald Trump and his allies in Congress fund SNAP immediately and end this government shutdown?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119030300/581696026/593712779?source=SBD_VF_EM_PET_111025_SNAP_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NjNkOTk4Yy02M2JlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=29sBR0XXWUKGqIE_fo2L_QZM9br41vxgw_ld6ubJc8U=&emci=4eccb088-8fba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=763d998c-63be-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=799895)** | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119030303/581696029/593712779?source=SBD_VF_EM_PET_111025_SNAP_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NjNkOTk4Yy02M2JlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=29sBR0XXWUKGqIE_fo2L_QZM9br41vxgw_ld6ubJc8U=&emci=4eccb088-8fba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=763d998c-63be-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=799895)

name: Ro Khanna
subject: BREAKING NEWS: I’m calling on Chuck Schumer to be replaced
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Peter,
BREAKING NEWS: Last night, I became the first member of Congress to call on Chuck Schumer to be replaced as Minority Leader for caving to Donald Trump.
If you’re not willing to fight to stop premium hikes on 20 million Americans , then what are you willing to fight for?
The Democratic Party needs to be strong. We need leadership that will fight for working people, not fold under pressure from Trump or corporate donors.
Sign if you agree: It’s time for Senate Democrats to replace Chuck Schumer as Minority Leader and elect someone who will stand up to Trump and protect healthcare. [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer]
https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer]
The donors and establishment won’t like me speaking out, but I’m confident that you will have my back. Will you stand with me?
Add your name now: Tell Democrats we need new leadership in the Senate that will fight for working people and protect our healthcare. [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer]
We cannot defeat extremism with weakness or compromise. We need courage, conviction, and clarity of purpose.
SIGN THE PETITION [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/replaceschumer]
Thank you for your activism and all that you do.
In solidarity,
Ro
Wait! One more thing–
Ro doesn't accept a cent from lobbyists or PACs, meaning our work is completely funded by grassroots supporters like you. By donating just $3 today, you’re helping us create a country that works for everyone, not just those at the top. That means a renewed investment in American manufacturing, Medicare for All, student debt relief, and a green future.
If you want to support our people-powered campaign as we continue to fight for progress in Washington, please consider making a donation of $3 or more today. Thank you!

name: Mark Warner
subject: Why I voted no.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
I’d like to take a moment to explain my vote.

|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119030779/581697547/-165121341?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_ShutdownVote_251110_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYTk1NWQwMy02NWJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=t_3SqPjcGL0YUx8lGWKJQYMa_DXdHtwNGphYUE0Jg1U=) Derek –

Late last night, the Senate advanced a continuing resolution to fund the government through January 30th.

**I voted no because this legislation does _nothing_ to address the Republican health care crisis – and without swift action, many Virginians will see their health care premiums double.**

Make no mistake: this is not a vote I took lightly.

In my time in politics, I’ve always believed in the importance of cutting bipartisan deals. And until this CR, I had been a part of every bipartisan deal made in the Senate in the past decade.

Instead of coming to the table to negotiate a long-term deal to address the health care crisis of his own making, Donald Trump did everything in his power to inflict the maximum amount of pain on Americans during this shutdown.

Every day this shutdown drags on, Virginians feel it.

Federal workers are struggling to pay their bills. Families are unsure how they will put food on the table because this administration is cruelly and deliberately withholding the assistance they need.

I want nothing more than to reopen the government, get folks back to work, and end the needless hardship this Republican shutdown is causing.

**But it comes down to this: we owe the American people more than a short-term fix that leaves working families staring down a health care crisis, and simply kicking the can down the road is not good enough.**

More soon,

Mark Warner [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119030780/581697548/-165121341?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_ShutdownVote_251110_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYTk1NWQwMy02NWJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=t_3SqPjcGL0YUx8lGWKJQYMa_DXdHtwNGphYUE0Jg1U=)

clean_body: I’d like to take a moment to explain my vote. | | | = Derek – Late last night, the Senate advanced a continuing resolution to fund the government through January 30th. I voted no because this legislation does nothing to address the Republican health care crisis – and without swift action, many Virginians will see their health care premiums double. Make no mistake: this is not a vote I took lightly. In my time in politics, I’ve always believed in the importance of cutting bipartisan deals. And until this CR, I had been a part of every bipartisan deal made in the Senate in the past decade. Instead of coming to the table to negotiate a long-term deal to address the health care crisis of his own making, Donald Trump did everything in his power to inflict the maximum amount of pain on Americans during this shutdown. Every day this shutdown drags on, Virginians feel it. Federal workers are struggling to pay their bills. Families are unsure how they will put food on the table because this administration is cruelly and deliberately withholding the assistance they need. I want nothing more than to reopen the government, get folks back to work, and end the needless hardship this Republican shutdown is causing. But it comes down to this: we owe the American people more than a short-term fix that leaves working families staring down a health care crisis, and simply kicking the can down the road is not good enough. everyone—one where communities have leaders who reflect them, where

name: Bob MacGuffie
subject: MAGA-GOP Would Dismiss the Mamdani Effect at Their... and Our Peril
body:
| | | | |
---
| \-- Reporting from the Political Front --
---
---
| | November 10, 2025
Last week's election of Zohran Mamdani was indeed a flex of the American Left's organizational prowess in the big city. But aside from the derisive comments and ridicule leveled from the Right - all justified in light of the history of similar collectivist movements - how this 34-year old got from unknown to mayor-elect in a year, warrants close examination as the curtain goes up on the critical 2026 election cycle. The American Right cannot afford to ignore the critically important lessons in the post-mortem of this improbable election. I took an initial cut at illuminating those lessons with insights in my article in today's [Connecticut Centinal](https://lk9rohfbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001tc37iVduRDap889qHKBJKZWuIkWto5Na3MbrBDqZB9dJ6NpGNRAwZ2_7So0OYRvHcsXR5MRAQQn6HOcF9Q_mEvm9fZq-6YuTznmutsrYc4Gznyhh0FdJayhu5y6gtMQka6Igq-58GdwqLnALc7Kic8BbAFUQ-sbT8MHEmGklxzfbRV8S53RedmS3TAA8jrPGiWZmOmYf0nTyhcm3NiCHr7dCST0SkJFaITx6AegBi9lvBQm2gguDlQ3NgTj3Ui8i&c=z4-wwWQ4XteYPv81DRq4ZofyNwzuAFZ4wEISUosbSklEYk_2einmfA==&ch=gyNUuO6wvKnDYtu1u1HlmeFhBcrJZ6HrR9Dn2WjGwhXT6Ts2H-b5oQ==), which also appears directly below.
MAGA-GOP Would Dismiss the Mamdani Effect at Their... and Our Peril By Bob MacGuffie
We on the Right certainly see Zohran Mamdani for what he is - a self-revealed Marxist and Islamist who has improbably captured the mayoralty of NYC in the 2025 election. We also recognize that those ideologies to their core are antithetical to our nation's founding principles and morality, and will inevitably be fatally corrosive to society in New York, and indeed well-beyond. Criticism and invective have been hurled at the man for what he represents and no-doubt, more will deservedly follow as events develop. No surprise to any of us acquainted with history. Though his ideologies are easy to ridicule and dismiss, we should examine the ideas and coalition which propelled this 34-year-old to his stunning victory, and the warning contained within them. Revealingly, the quandary of Mamdani's coalition mirrors that which Trump identified and assembled in his rise to power.
---
|
---
In short, Mamdani's coalition was not primarily that of the low-educated aggrieved, misfits, and revolutionary wannabees - though these types can be seen sprinkled throughout his rallies in ample quantities. Yes, he flipped back working-class Black and Latino neighborhoods - the types that responded to Trump's famous "what have you got to lose." And Mamdani's well-received message there can travel far beyond Brooklyn. But the key to his city-wide appeal was his huge (60%-70%) margins in the gentrified neighborhoods across the city. These are neighborhoods of educated young professional people earning roughly $80,000 to $150,000 a year. In a very illuminating article, Breitbart's Business Digest examined the phenomenon and termed this new, highly significant voting class the ["precarious professionals"](https://lk9rohfbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001tc37iVduRDap889qHKBJKZWuIkWto5Na3MbrBDqZB9dJ6NpGNRAwZ2_7So0OYRvHgRiKyphlwq_a1TpXgEm6ZEj_cU9PxrrDrhyGt5YBDtEzalVs-buIfZt0QCpcTUeHcBkl-n9jmGwAUMcN6Dnl1mn9vsDc8SR1usl79scRRHGXwwYVBEQnw1W7W7SeFvgr1Umkt7xKfMK6FGk-EdjZcmXNmj7my0OBwTFIXMxLtvRatrj9_txj0q5c3kRaBIC_NxP16ckGun6X5Wc5gfsGxT7-bD50tMtCdzK-Zlu9TXqh8lmdLBvJJA_t_mFfzUPPopSTTZaDPxE=&c=z4-wwWQ4XteYPv81DRq4ZofyNwzuAFZ4wEISUosbSklEYk_2einmfA==&ch=gyNUuO6wvKnDYtu1u1HlmeFhBcrJZ6HrR9Dn2WjGwhXT6Ts2H-b5oQ==). We are all acquainted with precarious-professionals. They are in our extended families, our friends or neighbors' kids and many of our own children, and indeed may be reading this column. They're 24-40, got educated, got good jobs, seemingly good money, many married - 'playing by the rules,' as the saying goes. But they're renting an apartment, with no hope in their minds, of ever owning a house at today's eye-popping prices. They're spending their lives renting what their parents owned. If they're married and have a child, there's no room for a second in their rental unit, and their healthcare costs are strangling them. And you can find masses of them in most cities and suburban areas across the nation. They are middle-class professionals, and in the first quarter of this century they've seen housing costs explode, job security evaporate, career opportunities outsourced, and now they're threatened by AI. They and their communities are rapidly becoming the Rust Belt of this century. And just as the MAGA movement successfully brought in the blue-collar displaced, it makes perfect sense to bring in the precarious-professionals - they are a natural fit within the coalition. But, it is imperative that their predicament and their needs be addressed with intelligent and credible policy prescriptions - in the private sector vein. Democrat candidates who addressed this voting block in New Jersey and Virginia were "talking about affordability," but offered no more than cheap notions like "I will freeze utility costs" - and were awarded a majority of votes for such vapid gab. Republicans in 2026 must not simply ape such nonsense. Next year voters will already be seeing that the cheap, perennial pablum and tweaks do nothing to solve the underlying problems. The DNC strategists think they've picked the lock on Tuesday, and as the '26 mid-term cycle develops they will be beating the affordability drum, and laying the middle-class squeeze at the feet of Donald Trump and the MAGA Republicans. Trump, like Reagan in '80 and Clinton in '92, rode into office stressing the inflation and joblessness experienced under their predecessors. Reagan had fortunate timing in that his policy prescriptions visibly kicked-in with enough results in time for his reelection. Typically for Clinton, his economic hardship claims were another mis-direction, and the '90s took off on a productivity binge entirely of the private sector's making. But the economic squeeze facing the middle-class today dwarfs those prior episodes. In fact, this squeeze has been building, going back sixty years to the dawn of big-time deficit spending - the infamous 'guns and butter' days of the mid-1960s Johnson Administration. It was here that running wars through budget deficits and 'printing money,' became the standard operating procedure of our ruling political class. The result is that the American standard of living has declined ever since the early '70s, and it is being most acutely felt today - it has all come home to roost. Each year the U.S. annual budget deficit (currently about $2 trillion) gets financed through the issuance of new federal debt. The more currency you print via debt-issuance, the more you devalue the purchasing power of the dollars in circulation. And it was all turbo-charged at the turn of this century. In 2000 the national debt was $5 trillion. Today it is kicking $38 trillion. Mountains of new dollars have been printed, and the real-world effects from those wealthy enough to accumulate them and reinvest them, are demand-driven, out of control prices for most of the basics for middle-class life - most notably, housing. The money printing has severely impacted healthcare and higher education as well, but government intervention in those markets has also been a mighty cost-multiplier. Leftist Democrats are happy to pounce on this as if it was Trump's doing, and win some elections in the process. Those of the Mamdani stripe - committed Socialists and Communists - seek a fundamental, collectivist, transformation of the U.S. economy - and they're being heard. The danger here is real and immediate, because the Left has the attention of the precarious-professionals, it has harnessed their frustration and anger to some degree, and it's offering them prescriptions straight from the Marxist playbook. And because most all of them have been exposed to the indoctrination of the public school system over the past forty years, they don't know the history of the definitive failure of government-controlled economies - and in large numbers are cheering on the Socialist prescriptions. The challenge for MAGA and the Republican candidates in the highly consequential upcoming mid-terms, is to recognize those in the professional middle-class squeeze, and connect with them, presenting free-market oriented, non-government solutions. A mighty tall task with less than a year to make the case and get traction. So far, President Trump's initial take on the election is off the mark. He's rightfully complaining that the corporate media doesn't report his successes - they never will. But, exit polling indicates voters are most concerned about "affordability," and on that front Trump is in denial - "everything is way down," he said - it is not. He promised the moon on affordability-related issues to get elected. This time Democrats campaigned griping about it, and voters have largely held Republicans accountable for the current state of affairs. Trump IS endeavoring to make the necessary economic structural shifts to bring about stable prices and a rise in the standard of living, but by their nature the cure is long-term - it did take decades to get into this mess. (We'll look at some solid specific policy proposals which address the intractable problems in a follow-up column.) Unfortunately, American voters are mighty impatient - all to their collective detriment. The vast majority of voters are unaware of the causes of the decades-long decline in their standard of living - from the time when one household breadwinner with an average job could support a household that today takes two highly-compensated, seasoned professionals to strive to maintain. Since our colleges still refuse to do it, candidates should sprinkle a little history of the crisis's true causes into their stump speeches. For well over a century, in an effort to guard the power of the ruling political class, the people have been told by the politicos, the commentariat, and the mass corporate media to vote for what's going to benefit them personally - and to vote their 'pocketbook.' The purpose of these messages is to cleave the citizenry, so the two major parties can bark out slogans and wave their deceptive wands every two years, to corral enough voters into their respective corners to win elections. They win some and lose some, but the process assures the established ruling political class stays in control... and the citizenry never looks in unison to them as the true source of society's strife, turmoil and hardship. This cynical and debilitating paradigm has to change. We've had a year of reveling in Trump's incredible win and the counter-revolution it has triggered. Mamdani's proposals offer just more warmed-over 'managed-decline.' But his rise and everything the organized American Left represents, pose a mortal threat to our efforts to re-establish the country on its foundational principles and morals. For if we don't set our sails right for this election cycle, we will be thrust to the sidelines and worse, by the enemies of our great republic in all their Leftist manifestations.

---
---
---
| In Liberty,
Bob MacGuffie bobmac@rightprinciples.com
---
| |
---
| Read [The Seventh Crisis](https://lk9rohfbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001tc37iVduRDap889qHKBJKZWuIkWto5Na3MbrBDqZB9dJ6NpGNRAwZ6tVWqI3brGdrmJG8peaeHIz09v_oUi2YO7mLQZIspmlcz2bXfZ5Njg81xZ1l-JXDqDxnQGwQa2knjkxNEtNplKTrtImaIxklg==&c=z4-wwWQ4XteYPv81DRq4ZofyNwzuAFZ4wEISUosbSklEYk_2einmfA==&ch=gyNUuO6wvKnDYtu1u1HlmeFhBcrJZ6HrR9Dn2WjGwhXT6Ts2H-b5oQ==)
---
|
---
| | | | LibertyLine | Fairfield | Fairfield, CT 06824 US
---

name: Ruben Gallego
subject: You have every right to be angry
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
Early on this year, a lot of people made promises. “I will do anything I can to stop Trump,” or “We have to fight back with everything we’ve got.” I took those promises seriously, but I guess some of my colleagues didn’t.
#

Frankly, Peter, I'm pretty pissed off.
I suspect you are, too.
Early on this year, a lot of people made promises. “I will do anything I can to stop Trump,” or “We have to fight back with everything we’ve got.” I took those promises seriously, but I guess some of my colleagues didn’t.
The biggest tool we have in the minority is to withhold our consent. Make Trump own this. Make Trump go to court to take SNAP benefits from hungry kids. Make it clear that Democrats are the only ones trying to make health care more affordable, and that Trump and the GOP have no plan. Call their bluff.
We have to do that much, because Trump and the GOP put us in this situation. They are pitting working people against each other — a family at a food bank vs a family that can’t afford their health care. In the richest country in the world we don’t have to pick winners. We can fight for both, and we can win for both, but not like this.
This is tough, but you don’t beat a bully like Trump by giving up.
It’s kind of fitting that today is the 250th birthday of my beloved Marine Corps. 250 years of fighting against tyranny. Maybe it’s just the Marine in me, but I am always going to fight like hell for our country, for our democracy, and for our people.
**Retreat? Hell! I just got here.**
[**I’m not giving up, and if you aren’t either, let me know. Add your name here so I can tell who’s ready to keep fighting, because we have a lot more work to do between now and next November.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/signup/pet_em_251110_shutdown/?source=em251110-1-120&akid=4032%2E734936%2E74C_oI)
[DON'T QUIT](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/signup/pet_em_251110_shutdown/?source=em251110-1-120&akid=4032%2E734936%2E74C_oI)
Semper Fi,
Ruben

Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
