name: Eric H. Holder, Jr.
subject: We will protect democracy in every part of our nation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
We just saw a clear indication of the power of the people

---
Peter, As I reflect on the results of last week’s election, one thing is clear: **The condition of our democracy is a paramount concern to the American people. It’s on people like us to do everything we can to protect it.** In California, the voters saw Donald Trump’s gerrymandering effort for what it is: A real threat to draw them — the people — out of a meaningful role in the electoral process. Their opposition was overwhelming. Californians voted in favor of a response to combat this threat and protect the voting power of the American people by double digits, and that effort stands in stark contrast to feckless Governor Abbott and other Republicans who ignored the overwhelming opposition by Texans to enact the state’s new, even more egregious gerrymander. **This should serve as a warning to Republicans who are considering heeding the continuing demands of the White House: It is politically unwise to go against principle and their constituents.** If necessary — and with the support of the people — more states will follow California’s lead, acting swiftly and decisively to protect Americans’ voice in Congress. **Here is the lesson I draw from last Tuesday:[We can and will protect democracy in every part of our nation.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119036743/581719324/-1263916904?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AGStatementNov4_email_20251110-NDRC-Tier1-AGStatementNov4_A&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251110-NDRC-Tier1-AGStatementNov4_A&utm_campaign=AGStatementNov4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjU2NTk3NzNiLTc4YmUtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMjI0ODIzZmY5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WnLiXDkgRn8u5qRODu4TjyWuCaJFO-nMJ9Vh0O8NYJc=)** And we must build upon the successes of this year — in California, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and beyond — to defend our nation in the trying months ahead. In solidarity, Eric H. Holder, Jr.

clean_body: We just saw a clear indication of the power of the people Peter, As I reflect on the results of last week’s election, one thing is clear: The condition of our democracy is a paramount concern to the American people. It’s on people like us to do everything we can to protect it. In California, the voters saw Donald Trump’s gerrymandering effort for what it is: A real threat to draw them — the people — out of a meaningful role in the electoral process. Their opposition was overwhelming. Californians voted in favor of a response to combat this threat and protect the voting power of the American people by double digits, and that effort stands in stark contrast to feckless Governor Abbott and other Republicans who ignored the overwhelming opposition by Texans to enact the state’s new, even more egregious gerrymander. This should serve as a warning to Republicans who are considering heeding the continuing demands of the White House: It is politically unwise to go against principle and their constituents. If necessary — and with the support of the people — more states will follow California’s lead, acting swiftly and decisively to protect Americans’ voice in Congress. Here is the lesson I draw from last Tuesday:= And we must build upon the successes of this year — in California, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and beyond — to defend our nation in the trying months ahead. In solidarity, Eric H. null, have you seen the news out of Michigan?

The National Republican Senatorial Committee (NRSC) told top Republican megadonors that Michigan is their top target for flipping the Senate in 2026.
So before we go any further, we have to ask: **[Will you rush $5 right now to help us defend this battleground seat and keep Michigan blue?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8015?t=4&akid=1290%2E49239%2EIGEeAh)**
| | [Donate](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8014?t=5&akid=1290%2E49239%2EIGEeAh)
---
✅ **The Good News:**

* Haley Stevens raised more than any other Democrat in the Michigan Senate race last quarter – a clear sign of grassroots momentum.

* Polling shows Haley is the only Democrat consistently beating MAGA Republican Mike Rogers in head-to-head matchups.

**⚠️ The Bad News:**
* Axios reports the National Republican Senatorial Committee just labeled Michigan as the "starting point" for expanding Senate Republicans' majority.

* They say Michigan is their #1 offensive target in the country for 2026.

* MAGA Republican Mike Rogers has more cash on hand than he did at this point in 2023 – and he only lost by 0.3% last cycle.

* The NRSC, Trump, and GOP megadonors have already rallied around Rogers, giving him a head start.

This is real. This is urgent. **But Michigan is winnable – if we fight back now.**

Team Haley is building the statewide infrastructure it will take to win this race, defeat MAGA Mike Rogers, and flip the Senate. But we can’t do it without you.
**[Will you rush a grassroots donation now to help us reach voters and protect this must-win Senate seat?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8014?t=6&akid=1290%2E49239%2EIGEeAh)**

Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
Boebert’s racist costume goes way beyond just another right wing controversy
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001uUDE8Anh5K6d02PiosAPJYcjat1utnzEC84SgHI-EYy_EmWO7cI8wDvCDqD-Q-yONU_82ZyUPL0KWPIwV7Ftmi6csQ5iK7F9kTV8JZlRNiOsn3GT0rLX0xWuWxAN2W16XxiD8phUY2dXqYQgze_xyxlW5WKKIoO16kJ8pFYstfWKWimoDw990Q0OyJCRXhq8jsxFX6ZJqj3Qa1VfHILQRi2ivYTVH4iz&c=AJPyf5BEycjsqThwckLHCR4blVQq8qKVQnQggLuQskzJgyicdbqCBg==&ch=V1lIcVoug_t9UiWDB54nlp8BHo6vEd5XqESNq3kQtOvnb3qISclJSA==)
---
|

Peter,
Lauren Boebert and her boyfriend had fun on Halloween. They mocked Mexican immigrants and celebrated ICE cruelty.
Beyond being simply a disgusting thing to do, Boebert's racism is bad politics. The population of this district is about 16% Hispanic or Latino. Most Latinos here have Mexican heritage.
The condemnation in Colorado has been swift, with groups that advocate for Latinos nearly universally calling for Boebert's resignation and apology.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001uUDE8Anh5K6d02PiosAPJYcjat1utnzEC84SgHI-EYy_EmWO7cI8wDvCDqD-Q-yONU_82ZyUPL0KWPIwV7Ftmi6csQ5iK7F9kTV8JZlRNiOsn3GT0rLX0xWuWxAN2W16XxiD8phUY2dXqYQgze_xyxlW5WKKIoO16kJ8pFYstfWKWimoDw990Q0OyJCRXhq8jsxFX6ZJqj3Qa1VfHILQRmnT6LHVpTZ2&c=AJPyf5BEycjsqThwckLHCR4blVQq8qKVQnQggLuQskzJgyicdbqCBg==&ch=V1lIcVoug_t9UiWDB54nlp8BHo6vEd5XqESNq3kQtOvnb3qISclJSA==)
---
Carpetbagger Boebert wouldn't know that her white supremacist attitude is a bad fit for this district: she just moved here to run in the open 4th district seat. She then went on to badly underperform in the 2024 election--receiving the least amount of votes of any Republican in this district ever while I set a new record for Democrats. And since then, she's been too chicken to face us in a real townhall.
Boebert's vile actions are turning off even conservative voters at the exact time American farmers and cattle ranchers are facing dire circumstances.
I was born here, raised in eastern Colorado and Douglas County. I have been working my entire life to bring people together and fight to make life better for everyone.
People are ready for change. It's time to come together to finally rid the nation of the embarrassment that is Lauren Boebert. name: Tim Kaine
subject: Reopening the government
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
Friends,
Last night I voted for a deal to reopen the government, and I want you to hear directly from me about why.
I said from the start that to earn my vote, we needed to be on a path toward fixing Republicans’ health care mess, and we needed to protect the federal workforce.
I voted “no” with my Democratic colleagues for 40 days because the GOP didn’t put anything on the table I could in good conscience vote “yes” on.
But over those 40 days, 2 million federal employees — and more than 300,000 in Virginia — were suffering. They missed two paychecks, with another around the corner. These workers, along with the millions of federal contractors who have been going without pay, have families that depend on them.
Beyond the pain of missing paychecks, hundreds of thousands of furloughed workers were wondering if they were next in line to be fired by Trump.
Tens of millions of SNAP recipients were going without benefits they rely on to put food on the table.
Air traffic was suffering nationwide — with real safety dangers.
And after 40 days, there was still no path to an Affordable Care Act vote. Republicans would not even debate an ACA fix until the government reopened — period.
So we negotiated for what we could get.
Last night’s vote paved the way to reopen the government with robust full-year funding for SNAP, WIC, and other important safety net services — because we just can’t let people go hungry.
All furloughed workers will return to work with backpay — so people can pay their mortgages, rent, and heating bills.
All the federal workers who Trump baselessly fired will be reinstated — and no federal agencies will be allowed to conduct mass layoffs for the duration of this funding bill.
Since Trump’s second term began, our federal workers have been constantly trashed. Tormented. Illegally fired. This deal is a big win for them.
Finally, last night’s vote gave us a path for an ACA fix to stop a devastating rise in premiums — something Republicans weren’t even willing to consider before last night. It’s not a guarantee, but I believe we can win this critical ACA battle if we fight for it together. We won’t win it if we turn to in-fighting.
And at a bare minimum, this fight will show the American people with complete clarity who is standing with them to make health care affordable, and who isn’t. That’s the real fight we need to focus on in 2026.
As we move forward, I will keep working toward a long-term government spending plan that supports Virginians and all Americans.
Thanks for reading.
—Tim Kaine
Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #272
Williamsburg, VA 23185
Emails like this one are a critical part of staying in touch during this important election year. We value your support, and are so glad to have you on the team. subject: Lauren Boebert’s racist insults are backfiring spectacularly
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
Boebert’s racist costume goes way beyond just another right wing controversy
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Tn9oBDhm3jQeT5mKsYPD45kSI9vGpdKo-A3I-ovL5XYpT6Z4kAXDlAPfL2DUys_AOrzxGJRcUJQ0g8FzEQOQbvBheWa6GL1UN-pn1I5VTLZ0lfqgD-ydqbPFvLtu3fbYuLNl-VYpJP1bMUGEBI8Jw3HwnIluCaeMC57gHfu7mhCtfkO8V-3i95Nr7FoDV7cqzUOuvSQcXeFZiIREjF6MYsaUyGUTsfpQ&c=yBu2URhp_MoLuoMliwza4IKZt9gNrFfK8VT7JWkHIWz-LfXaNrYx7A==&ch=SYoYgrKOzVaEDJ6nkmcxEgAWp26baau9b3_ElpyYlTGRQ5SczsHAmQ==)

|

Peter,
Lauren Boebert and her boyfriend had fun on Halloween. They mocked Mexican immigrants and celebrated ICE cruelty.
Beyond being simply a disgusting thing to do, Boebert's racism is bad politics. The population of this district is about 16% Hispanic or Latino. Most Latinos here have Mexican heritage.
The condemnation in Colorado has been swift, with groups that advocate for Latinos nearly universally calling for Boebert's resignation and apology.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Tn9oBDhm3jQeT5mKsYPD45kSI9vGpdKo-A3I-ovL5XYpT6Z4kAXDlAPfL2DUys_AOrzxGJRcUJQ0g8FzEQOQbvBheWa6GL1UN-pn1I5VTLZ0lfqgD-ydqbPFvLtu3fbYuLNl-VYpJP1bMUGEBI8Jw3HwnIluCaeMC57gHfu7mhCtfkO8V-3i95Nr7FoDV7cqzUOuvSQcXeFZiIREjF6MYhd92VcdI2Xb&c=yBu2URhp_MoLuoMliwza4IKZt9gNrFfK8VT7JWkHIWz-LfXaNrYx7A==&ch=SYoYgrKOzVaEDJ6nkmcxEgAWp26baau9b3_ElpyYlTGRQ5SczsHAmQ==)
---
Carpetbagger Boebert wouldn't know that her white supremacist attitude is a bad fit for this district: she just moved here to run in the open 4th district seat. She then went on to badly underperform in the 2024 election--receiving the least amount of votes of any Republican in this district ever while I set a new record for Democrats. And since then, she's been too chicken to face us in a real townhall.
Boebert's vile actions are turning off even conservative voters at the exact time American farmers and cattle ranchers are facing dire circumstances.
I was born here, raised in eastern Colorado and Douglas County. I have been working my entire life to bring people together and fight to make life better for everyone.
People are ready for change. It's time to come together to finally rid the nation of the embarrassment that is Lauren Boebert. And since then, she's been too chicken to face us in a real townhall. Boebert's vile actions are turning off even conservative voters at the exact time American farmers and cattle ranchers are facing dire circumstances. I was born here, raised in eastern Colorado and Douglas County. I have been working my entire life to bring people together and fight to make life better for everyone. People are ready for change. It's time to come together to finally rid the nation of the embarrassment that is Lauren Boebert. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | Every time Boebert lands herself in the news with clownish and shameful behavior like this, she loses more support. And when that happens--which is often--our team is on the ground winning voters who are fed up with politicians like Boebert. Thank you for joining our team. Trisha Calvarese Democrat for CO-04 | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | | | | | | |

Together, we the people, are building a future where everyone is free to live, speak, and be themselves. A future where our communities have what they need to thrive regardless of their background. And while billionaires work to divide us, we're building bridges and organizing with our neighbors.
Our new _Powered by the People_ logo and merch designs were created in partnership with _The South_ , a lifestyle branding company by Angela Peoples and Kevin Banatte that showcases unapologetic Black culture.
Because every community has great swag, this new streetwear is something we can and should wear _proudly._ By repping Powered by the People, you can help make our message be heard far and wide and bring more young people into our movement.
**After all, young people and working people power our country, our workplaces, our communities, and each other.**
****

name: Michael Bennet
subject: Can I be honest?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119037092/581720251/-2054079050?code=jtk979-su-nd&refcode=jtk979-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNjU0YWNlOS03OGJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=f41OTRkhDCLOKW54PPFE9OCimOEihV3o3nCyR19AmEU=)
---
Can I be honest, friend?
One of my least favorite parts about running for office is asking folks like you for your hard-earned money. I'm sure you dislike getting those emails just as much.
Well, we're both in luck, because I'm not reaching out for donations – _[I just need your personal endorsement of me in Colorado's governor race.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119037093/581720252/172444050?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk979-su-nd&refcode=jtk979-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNjU0YWNlOS03OGJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=f41OTRkhDCLOKW54PPFE9OCimOEihV3o3nCyR19AmEU=&emci=62a88789-53be-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=0654ace9-78be-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=1295540)_
| | [Endorse Michael Bennet →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119037094/581720253/172444050?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk979-su-nd&refcode=jtk979-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNjU0YWNlOS03OGJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=f41OTRkhDCLOKW54PPFE9OCimOEihV3o3nCyR19AmEU=&emci=62a88789-53be-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=0654ace9-78be-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=1295540)
---
And here's why:
From unaffordable housing to inaccessible health care and struggling schools, Colorado faces real problems. But I know solutions are within our reach.
I've spent years working to deliver change for Colorado from the U.S. Senate. And after traveling to every corner of our state, building relationships and convening Coloradans of all stripes to take on our greatest challenges, together, we've succeeded – by passing the Affordable Care Act, expanding the Child Tax Credit, investing in high-speed internet, upgrading our roads and bridges, and capping prescription drug costs for seniors.
But given Trump's thirst for power and the cowardly partisanship that's infected his party, it's clear that the best solutions to our problems _will not_ come from the broken politics or Trump’s chaos in D.C. They have to start here in Colorado. **And that's why I'm running.**
I'm running for governor to rebuild our middle class and to build an economy that works for everyone, not just the people at the top.
I'm running to ensure we set the nationwide example of how to really protect working families from Trump's costly tariffs and cruel agenda.
**I'm running because I know that together, we can create lasting solutions to our shared challenges, and make Colorado the best place to live and raise a family – but to make that happen, I need you with me.**

clean_body: | | | = Can I be honest, friend? One of my least favorite parts about running for office is asking folks like you for your hard-earned money. I'm sure you dislike getting those emails just as much. Well, we're both in luck, because I'm not reaching out for donations – | | And here's why: From unaffordable housing to inaccessible health care and struggling schools, Colorado faces real problems. But I know solutions are within our reach. I've spent years working to deliver change for Colorado from the U.S. Senate. And after traveling to every corner of our state, building relationships and convening Coloradans of all stripes to take on our greatest challenges, together, we've succeeded – by passing the Affordable Care Act, expanding the Child Tax Credit, investing in high-speed internet, upgrading our roads and bridges, and capping prescription drug costs for seniors. But given Trump's thirst for power and the cowardly partisanship that's infected his party, it's clear that the best solutions to our problems will not come from the broken politics or Trump’s chaos in D.C. They have to start here in Colorado. And that's why I'm running. I'm running for governor to rebuild our middle class and to build an economy that works for everyone, not just the people at the top. I'm running to ensure we set the nationwide example of how to really protect working families from Trump's costly tariffs and cruel agenda. I'm running because I know that together, we can create lasting solutions to our shared challenges, and make Colorado the best place to live and raise a family – but to make that happen, I need you with me. | | Whether you've been here from the very beginning or just found out about my campaign yesterday, thank you for being a part of this team. name: Bohannan HQ
subject: Mariannette's been living under a rock
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
"Iowa farmers and rural communities are paying the price when the administration chooses a chaotic trade policy."

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119037240/581720865/-975735282?refcode=em_logoheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MWZkMmEyZS03YWJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zlurNfJ369ufTM-p41MgKHGH9Q32nRhTH5bOBvSmUUE=)
Peter, has Mariannette Miller-Meeks been living under a rock? We think so!

In her recent tele town hall, she was asked if she still supported the tariffs and the harm they're causing to Iowa's farmers.

**Mariannette Miller-Meeks responded that "she hasn't heard from any farmers who have felt betrayed by tariffs" and proceeded to spew out political jargon to try and change the subject.**

We find it hard to believe she hasn't heard anything from her constituents or seen the statement from the **Iowa Soybean Association pleading for support and an end to the tariffs.**

Or this direct quote from the Iowa Farmers Union: **"Iowa farmers and rural communities are paying the price when the administration chooses a chaotic trade policy."**

No matter how she tries to spin it or avoid the question, the tariffs Mariannette Miller-Meeks has supported over and over again are hurting our farmers by making their livelihoods more expensive and driving away key trade partners.

Mariannette Miller-Meeks has made it clear that when Iowans speak, she's all too happy to cover her ears and go along with the wishes of party bosses. [**Christina is ready to step up and be the leader Mariannette Miller-Meeks will never be. She is ready to listen to her constituents' concerns and fight for them in the halls of Congress. Help her get there by making a donation to her campaign today >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119037241/581720866/-1610961223?refcode=em25_251110_Tariff_fnd_gq_act___BHQ_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251110_Tariff_fnd_gq_act___BHQ_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=61fd2a2e-7abe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MWZkMmEyZS03YWJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zlurNfJ369ufTM-p41MgKHGH9Q32nRhTH5bOBvSmUUE=)

clean_body: "Iowa farmers and rural communities are paying the price when the administration chooses a chaotic trade policy." | = Peter, has Mariannette Miller-Meeks been living under a rock? We think so! In her recent tele town hall, she was asked if she still supported the tariffs and the harm they're causing to Iowa's farmers. Mariannette Miller-Meeks responded that "she hasn't heard from any farmers who have felt betrayed by tariffs" and proceeded to spew out political jargon to try and change the subject. We find it hard to believe she hasn't heard anything from her constituents or seen the statement from the Iowa Soybean Association pleading for support and an end to the tariffs. Or this direct quote from the Iowa Farmers Union: "Iowa farmers and rural communities are paying the price when the administration chooses a chaotic trade policy." No matter how she tries to spin it or avoid the question, the tariffs Mariannette Miller-Meeks has supported over and over again are hurting our farmers by making their livelihoods more expensive and driving away key trade partners. Mariannette Miller-Meeks has made it clear that when Iowans speak, she's all too happy to cover her ears and go along with the wishes of party bosses. = If you've saved your payment information with Peter,

From California to New York, Virginia to New Jersey, all the way down to Georgia, voters across the country sent a clear message: **They're sick and tired of the Republican status quo.**

They want real leadership—leaders who put people over politics, solutions over obstruction, and families over party lines and self-serving agendas. _And they made sure to vote like they meant it._

This momentum is exactly what we need as we head into 2026 and the upcoming midterm elections. But it's on us to make sure it continues–into every race, every campaign, and every conversation. [**Will you chip in $5, or whatever you can, to keep our movement strong and growing in 2026 and beyond? Peter, the American people have seen what leadership under Donald Trump and Republicans looks like, **and they've had enough.**

We watched Republicans pass a Big, Ugly Bill that decimated healthcare for millions of Americans while handing massive tax breaks to billionaires. We're seeing them in real time actively pushing for states to enact abortion bans, manipulating congressional districts to silence Democratic voices, and even going after vote-by-mail to ensure they keep hold of their power. Not to mention, we're in the longest government shutdown in history because Republicans refuse to come to work and devise a plan with Democrats that saves the American people from debilitating healthcare costs.

Democrats are offering something different: leaders who put people over politics, solutions over obstruction, and communities over party lines. Because this is exactly the kind of leadership the American people are asking for.

Next November, we have a chance to get our country back on track. We have a chance to flip the House, keep WA-08 blue, and fill Congress with leaders who will always work toward progress. But it's going to take all of us working together to get the job done. [**Will you join the fight for a blue Congress in 2026 by chipping in to Kim's campaign today?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119037431/581721448/1491818712?refcode=em25_251110_result_fndd_gq_act___TS_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251110_result_fndd_gq_act___TS_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=5afe5196-7abe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWZlNTE5Ni03YWJlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HEwZA5uS9ZTAOj6Bl7YypCiGDxFWzigcDkGQpIq4rsM=)

clean_body: From California to New York, Virginia to New Jersey, all the way down to Georgia, voters across the country sent a clear message: Peter, From California to New York, Virginia to New Jersey, all the way down to Georgia, voters across the country sent a clear message: They're sick and tired of the Republican status quo. They want real leadership—leaders who put people over politics, solutions over obstruction, and families over party lines and self-serving agendas. And they made sure to vote like they meant it. This momentum is exactly what we need as we head into 2026 and the upcoming midterm elections. But it's on us to make sure it continues–into every race, every campaign, and every conversation. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Peter, the American people have seen what leadership under Donald Trump and Republicans looks like, and they've had enough. We watched Republicans pass a Big, Ugly Bill that decimated healthcare for millions of Americans while handing massive tax breaks to billionaires. We're seeing them in real time actively pushing for states to enact abortion bans, manipulating congressional districts to silence Democratic voices, and even going after vote-by-mail to ensure they keep hold of their power. Not to mention, we're in the longest government shutdown in history because Republicans refuse to come to work and devise a plan with Democrats that saves the American people from debilitating healthcare costs. Democrats are offering something different: leaders who put people over politics, solutions over obstruction, and communities over party lines. Because this is exactly the kind of leadership the American people are asking for. Next November, we have a chance to get our country back on track. We have a chance to flip the House, keep WA-08 blue, and fill Congress with leaders who will always work toward progress. But it's going to take all of us working together to get the job done. = Thank you, Team Schrier Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. Chip in now to help protect Kim's seat. = Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress PO Box 2728 Issaquah, WA 98027 United States

name: Tobias Read
subject: “Like living in hell”: President Trump says Portland will be next city to see National Guard on streets
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251110em-portland-pet-rs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251110em-portland-pet-rs]
Hi, it’s Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read.
After weeks of attacking our state, President Trump ordered troops to Portland.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251104-portland-pet-rs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251104-portland-pet-rs]
In the weeks leading up to this decision, he has stood in the Oval Office and called Portland “like living in hell.” He has even attacked our neighbors as “paid terrorists.”
The Portland I know is a beautiful, vibrant, and resilient city. It’s a place where families build their futures, where communities come together, and where people stand up for justice.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251104-portland-pet-rs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251104-portland-pet-rs]
Like many other major cities across the country, Portland has faced its share of challenges. Denying this doesn’t help move Portland and Oregon forward.
At the same time, Portland is a city worth supporting — not demonizing.
Let me be clear: we do not need President Trump’s troops in Oregon. And his threats have nothing to do with protecting Oregonians. We do not need fear-mongering from Washington. What we need is support for our communities, respect for our rights, and pride in all that makes Portland strong.
That’s why I’m asking you to join me: Add your name today to say you love Portland and you reject President Trump’s hateful, divisive rhetoric. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/LnPIG_b6_0G7mo74o3mtCQ2]
ADD YOUR NAME: I ♥️ PORTLAND [https://secure.ngpvan.com/LnPIG_b6_0G7mo74o3mtCQ2]
Oregon will always rise to meet the moment. And no matter what threats come from Washington, we will stand together, protect our communities, and defend our state’s values.
Thank you,
Tobias Read
Secretary of State, Oregon
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251110em-portland-pet-rs]
https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR [https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR] https://twitter.com/TobiasRead [https://twitter.com/TobiasRead] https://instagram.com/tobiasreador [https://instagram.com/tobiasreador]

Friends of Tobias Read (5208)
PO Box 42307
Portland, OR 97242
United States

name: AOC
subject: Shutdown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
“If Republicans have the majority, why are they blaming Democrats for the shutdown?”
#
Someone on Instagram recently asked me, “If Republicans have the majority, why are they blaming Democrats for the shutdown?”
Because they are bad leaders, and **they don’t take accountability for a damn thing**.
It’s kind of interesting, because Republicans are an anti-government party — they fundamentally do not believe in governance. That is why they’re all about cuts, ending things, stopping things, etc.
And so, the interesting part is that when you are an anti-government party, and win an election, you become the government — and **you don’t know what to do**.
You can’t lead, you can’t make decisions effectively, you can’t make government a force for good in people’s lives, so the corruption continues.
These guys don’t want to be in charge, they want to complain. Being in a place of perpetual grievance is their home base. The problem is that when you run on grievance, and then you have to cross a threshold into leadership — leadership is about ownership.
**Leadership is about, “Success is due to everybody, and failure is on who’s on top.”**
These folks are allergic to accountability. They are allergic to trying to get anything done. That’s why the only thing they can accomplish is shutting the government down.
You ask these guys, “Can you have an honest assessment of yourself, and name a single thing that could be done?” — they can’t give you an answer.
Everything is everybody else’s fault. It’s the most weenie stuff I’ve ever seen.
A party that’s in charge of the House, Senate, and Presidency — and complete partisan takeover of the Supreme Court — is going to somehow turn and tell people that anything is anything but their fault?
**Give me a break**.
**We deserve so much better** than leaders who will sell us out to give tax breaks to their billionaire donors.
[Will you make a $5 contribution today so that we can keep organizing working people in red, blue, and purple districts across the country? We must keep building this mass movement to win the future we deserve.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251110.1i9OQg&refcode=em20251110-10187-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10187%2E2526796%2Eh9UxcP)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251110.1i9OQg&refcode=em20251110-10187-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=10187%2E2526796%2Eh9UxcP)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20251110?refcode=em20251110-10187-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=10187_2526796_h9UxcP&akid=10187%2E2526796%2Eh9UxcP)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20251110-10187-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=10187%2E2526796%2Eh9UxcP)**

name: Jeff Merkley
subject: The vote to reopen the government with no deal on health care is a brutal blow to all of us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
Trump inflicted maximum pain on Americans by withholding SNAP benefits, canceling thousands of flights, and illegally firing federal workers.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119037729/581722713/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNTFlYjQ2Ni03Y2JlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GoGqhnA2jPMR4O1AZ5mBi9DBMqFkhBwmkjQsUIue1_M=&emci=1d0fc6d6-6fbe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=b51eb466-7cbe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=7731215)

| | The vote to reopen the government with no deal on health care is a brutal blow to all those who have stood together to save affordable health care for more than 20 million Americans. **I strived to stop this outcome.** I voted "NO" last night with every fiber of my being. Make no mistake, the blame lies with Trump and Congressional Republicans, who shut down the federal government because they refused to restore health care for millions of Americans after they savaged it in their Big Ugly Betrayal of a bill — all to fund tax breaks for billionaires. And in an unprecedented act of cruelty, Trump inflicted maximum pain on Americans by withholding SNAP benefits, canceling thousands of flights, and illegally firing federal workers. It is shameful that the entire Republican Party used hungry kids and our most vulnerable families as bargaining chips. Trump's authoritarian tyranny is destroying our democracy every day. **The only way to stop him is to stand up to him.** I will redouble my efforts to stop Donald Trump's authoritarian takeover, and to do it in partnership with grassroots champions who are organizing across our entire country. name: Cori Bush
subject: Democrats just caved. Again.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori For Us
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251110_demscave_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251110_demscave_act]
Peter,
Democrats just caved on the shutdown—and got nothing in return.
Here’s what just happened: a handful of Senate Democrats (most who don’t plan on running again) joined Republicans to vote yes on reopening the government, but offering zero help for families. It doesn’t restore Medicaid funding, protect Affordable Care Act subsidies, or stop healthcare premiums from skyrocketing for millions of Americans.
The Senate still has to vote, and the House will have to follow, so there’s still a chance to push back. But if this deal passes, it leaves Americans behind, once again. While politicians in Washington shrug, families back home are staring down medical bills they can’t pay and groceries they can’t afford.
I would say I can’t believe it, but that’s not true. I can. I’ve said it before, and I’ll say it again: this isn’t leadership. It’s surrender.
The way I flipped the tables in 2021 to stop mass evictions during the pandemic is the same way I’d be fighting right now, to protect your healthcare and your dignity.
St. Louis deserves a representative who’s willing to lay it all on the line. Because when people’s lives are on the line, you fight. You don’t fold.
If you’re tired of watching Democrats cave while Republicans gut our future, I need you with me today [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251110_demscave_act] . We’re building a movement that doesn’t back down, but we can’t do it without grassroots power.
Chip in $5, $10, or whatever you can right now to say you’re done with surrender and ready to fight for real change: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251110_demscave_act]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=x_251110_demscave_act] DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=x_251110_demscave_act]
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=x_251110_demscave_act] DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=x_251110_demscave_act]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=x_251110_demscave_act] OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?express_lane_other=true&refcode=x_251110_demscave_act]
In solidarity,
Cori
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251110_demscave_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251110_demscave_act] Cori Bush is a pastor, nurse, survivor, and formerly unhoused single mother of two who knows what it means to struggle and what it means to rise .
As a Congresswoman, she has always shown up when it’s hardest: securing disaster relief, lowering insulin costs, and delivering millions for our communities.
Across St. Louis, families are being priced out and pushed out. Cori Bush is fighting back for affordable housing, fair wages, and utilities as a human right. Be part of this people-first movement. Join Team Cori today and sign up to volunteer. [https://www.coribush.org]

subject: Due To The Topics Tonight, There Will Be NO YouTube Livestream
body:
Due to the nature of the topics to be discussed tonight at THE REVELATION CONVERSATION, it will not be live-streamed on Sheridan's YouTube channel. H
| | |
---
Due to the nature of the topics to be discussed tonight at **THE REVELATION CONVERSATION** , it _will not be_ live-streamed on Sheridan's YouTube channel. However, you can watch it on my [X](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26307296.13584.1.e2b370841adfc86cc0bbd07f785e91c8) account: [https://x.com/JacksonLahmeyer](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26307296.13585.1.c23f615d2f52dd98ede015bc0c0739e8) 6:30pm CT | FREE Event | LIVE Q&A **Mark Your Calendar's For These Upcoming Events At Sheridan:** -Sat, Nov 22 @5pm CT - **Sheridan Men's Gathering** -Sun, Nov 23 @10am CT - **74th Anniversary Service w/Darryl Strawberry** -Wed, Nov 26 @6:30pm - **Church Chili Cook-off** -Sun, Dec 21 @10am CT - **Christmas Celebration Service** -Wed, Dec 24 @4pm CT - **Christmas Eve Service** | Hymns, Candles + Communion -Wed, Dec 31 @6:30pm CT - **New Year's Eve Service** Please consider giving to the mission of Sheridan: https://sheridan.church/give/

Recently, I woke up today with a really cool notification... My **FIRST** book, ["_Chasing After The Wind_ "](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26307296.13589.1.4342639b3248c2986a467356ededf642) is out and has reached **#12 in Religion/ Christian Living / Personal Growth**! **I have been blown away by the response!** THANK YOU SO MUCH! If you have not ordered your copy you can still get the **Limited Edition Signed Book** here: _[*"Chasing After The Wind"*](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26307296.13590.1.e452f8fea4404b738cabbf1513e30cc7)_ It is also available on [Amazon](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26307296.13591.1.cd630f9c435bc021ddec8b34c7cf24dc)\+ [Barnes & Noble](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26307296.13592.1.9eb92482ef90e3ff363f52971b1a11d5). **Amazon** : [https://www.amazon.com/Chasing-After-Wind-Catching-Everybody/dp/B0F7M4GK26](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26307296.13593.1.3b2a3f2110e7da6960c5c149422cc140) **Barnes & Noble**: [https://www.barnesandnoble.com/w/chasing-after-the-wind-jackson-lahmeyer/1147391019](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26307296.13594.1.8e33502b12c021920a377f76a2c15617) I really want to thank **MLB LEGEND Darryl Strawberry** for writing the Foreword and becoming vulnerable by sharing his story. The Foreword is worth getting the book, I promise. This book is 200 pages and I am confident will change your life as it has mine. In ["Chasing After The Wind"](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26307296.13595.1.535155e0ad624baaf9f33daef52f1b99) I share VERY personal stories and I decided to get real and raw with the heartbreaks and triumphs I have experienced.

name: Jane Fonda
subject: My thoughts on last night’s Senate vote
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
We’re not just fighting Republicans. We’re clearly fighting for better Democrats.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119037831/581723081/1328794144?refcode=ea.46769a7a-7cbe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ms=ea.46769a7a-7cbe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ2NzY5YTdhLTdjYmUtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMjI0ODIzZmY5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=n59Y3NPsYsG-WlkI0gKroVQVO13jnI_JJ0uosFaGlDQ=&emci=fb9ecdfe-68be-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=46769a7a-7cbe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=4823105)
---
**Last night's Senate vote makes it plain. We’re not just fighting Republicans. We’re clearly fighting for better Democrats — the kind who have a backbone, who lead with courage, who do what’s right even when it’s not easy.** Disappointment doesn’t begin to describe what I’m feeling. I know many of you went to the polls on Tuesday to vote out the status quo. It’s painful to see that despite millions upon millions of voters making their voices heard, despite every contest being a referendum on Trump, his enablers, and their agenda of chaos, some of our elected officials still haven’t gotten the message. It was also a test for Democrats — a measure of what voters expect from their leaders, and who they’re willing to stand behind. We want brave leaders. We want bold leaders. We want this government shutdown to end, but not at the expense of working people and families who are struggling to make ends meet. So when 8 Democrats joined Republicans to vote for Trump’s “resolution” — one that could double or even triple healthcare premiums for more than 20 million Americans who rely on the Affordable Care Act — it felt like betrayal. Democrats need to understand there are real consequences when they fail to stand up to Trump. People aren’t just numbers or statistics; these are lives on the line. Lives that depend on their elected officials to fight for them. So hear me loud and clear when I say this: **JanePAC will always support candidates who have the courage to confront Donald Trump, and the powers and profiteers that enable him. That’s why we back candidates who show, not just say, that they’re ready to lead:**
* _Will they legislate with their communities in their hearts?_
* _Are they brave enough to stand up to Trump and Big Oil?_
* _Will they fight like hell for the people and our planet when it counts the most?_

The fact that we even have to ask ourselves these questions is precisely why, at the age of 87, I have no plans of slowing down. Change _is_ coming. I know it’s hard to believe that right now, but we’re putting Republicans AND Democrats on notice. We will never stop fighting for a government that serves people. Not power, not profit, not fear, but the very beginning of our Constitution: **_We the people._** In solidarity, Jane Fonda [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119037832/581723082/2105508926?refcode=ea.46769a7a-7cbe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ms=ea.46769a7a-7cbe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ2NzY5YTdhLTdjYmUtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMjI0ODIzZmY5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=n59Y3NPsYsG-WlkI0gKroVQVO13jnI_JJ0uosFaGlDQ=) | [**DONATE**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119037833/581723083/2105508926?refcode=ea.46769a7a-7cbe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ms=ea.46769a7a-7cbe-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ2NzY5YTdhLTdjYmUtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMjI0ODIzZmY5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=n59Y3NPsYsG-WlkI0gKroVQVO13jnI_JJ0uosFaGlDQ=)

name: Rep.-elect Adelita Grijalva (via Democrats.org)
subject: I’m the final vote Democrats need to make the Epstein files public:
body:
---
Derek, I’m Representative-elect Adelita Grijalva, and **I'm the final vote Democrats need to force a vote on releasing the Epstein files.** But it's been nearly seven weeks since I was elected to Congress. This is the longest a representative has _ever_ waited to be sworn in after winning a special election. We already know that Trump has lied about the files, and Democrats are so close to getting to the bottom of it. But we need your help to keep the pressure up. That starts with folks like you splitting a contribution between my campaign and the DNC to show Republicans that we will release the files, uncover their corruption, and kick them out of power in 2026. clean_body: Derek, I’m Representative-elect Adelita Grijalva, and I'm the final vote Democrats need to force a vote on releasing the Epstein files. But it's been nearly seven weeks since I was elected to Congress. This is the longest a representative has ever waited to be sworn in after winning a special election. We already know that Trump has lied about the files, and Democrats are so close to getting to the bottom of it. But we need your help to keep the pressure up. That starts with folks like you splitting a contribution between my campaign and the DNC to show Republicans that we will release the files, uncover their corruption, and kick them out of power in 2026. If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your split contribution between Adelita Grijalva and the Democratic National Committee instantly: | | | ** | | | | | | | ** Let’s get to the bottom of this. So believe me when I say that I'm deeply concerned right now.
My friend, Steve Scalise, just informed me that the top House Democrat Super PAC is already unloading $50,000,000.000 (and no, this ain't a typo) into key battleground districts. They're hitting us harder and earlier than ever before.
That's enough cash to completely overwhelm DOZENS of our most vulnerable Conservatives!
_And to make matters even worse, my buddies in the House have a HUGE deadline coming up, and I just got word they 're falling 15-20% behind their goal.__
_It 'll be a real disaster if they miss it.

They're hitting us harder and earlier than ever before. That's enough cash to completely overwhelm DOZENS of our most vulnerable Conservatives! | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: Something deeply troubling is unfolding in the Rust Belt’s forgotten towns—and now it’s all on camera.| | | | November 10 | | | | | Jaw-Dropping Video Documents What Locals Fear Most | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Something deeply troubling is unfolding in the Rust Belt’s forgotten towns—and now it’s all on camera. Locals quietly share stories of events so strange, many once chose to look away—until this footage forced the truth into the open. This chilling video documents what others refused to acknowledge. I spoke with a witness whose experience is truly unforgettable. If you think you know what’s happening in these places, prepare to see them in a whole new light. | | | 🟡 News From "American Alternative Assets" | (Sponsored) ---|--- Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Derek Tran
subject: This “deal” fails to help our community
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119038614/581726564/-1516755731?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODIyYWE1YTYtODBiZS1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAyMjQ4MjNmZjliIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ubs749lMz786SbZhWM2xwxzstW-gECsbC7BsdzS8Kuk=)
Friend,

Late last night, the Senate agreed to a deal to end the government shutdown. Eight Democratic Senators joined Republicans – despite the so-called deal not including an immediate vote on healthcare subsidies.

Instead, it delays a vote to December. It’s my belief that **if Republicans refuse to make access to healthcare a priority right now, they never will.**

We can’t afford to leave that up to chance. Not when millions are at risk of losing their coverage or seeing their payments double, triple, or even quadruple.

**The agreement proposed by the Senate fails to meet the urgent needs of our community** : affordable healthcare, food for children and families, small businesses, and more are all on the line.

Trump and the Republicans in Congress have consistently disregarded the needs of our communities – instead, using them as political pawns in their partisan agenda. Americans should not bear the brunt of their chaos.

Any deal that doesn’t immediately address the needs of the American people, especially rising healthcare costs, is a betrayal. Full stop.

I want you to know that if the Senate’s deal comes to a vote in the House, I will vote _no_. There’s more work to do, and Californians deserve better.

Thank you,

Derek

subject: It’s a pathetic promise, Derek
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRIS DELUZIO FOR CONGRESS
body:

For months, I’ve been hearing from folks bracing for their health insurance premiums to skyrocket. Some are scared they’ll lose coverage altogether because they can’t afford these price hikes. The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=21798%2E878179%2EzGWn45). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=21798%2E878179%2EzGWn45).**

I will be voting no this week and I want to explain why. Once again, Schumer and a handful of Senate Democrats surrendered when we needed them to fight. I genuinely do not understand what they are thinking - what was the point of holding out if absolutely nothing gets done on the healthcare crisis? My name is Theodis Daniel. ** ** Seven years ago, if you had told me President Trump would one day be swearing in my son as an honorary member of the U.S. Secret Service, **_I would 've never believed you_.** ** ** **It was around that time DJ was diagnosed with a rare form of brain and spinal cancer.** ** ** _My world was crushed._ As a single father, DJ was the shining light that made my life worth living. ** ** We went through MONTHS of treatment together... ** ** We did EVERYTHING the doctor told us to do... ** ** **But to this day,_I know it was God_** **_who saved my son_.** ** ** Fast forward seven years, and DJ's stronger than ever before. His story INSPIRED millions, and _even caught the attention of President Trump_. **That 's when DJ's biggest ****dream finally****came true.** _(You may remember watching this on TV)_ **At President Trump 's joint address to Congress, DJ was sworn in as an honorary member of the U.S. Secret Service.**

**That 's why I'm running ****for Congress.** ** ** The media wants you to believe President Trump is nothing but a "_heartless, ruthless, dictator._ " ** ** But I saw first hand that he is a _father, grandfather, and compassionate man_. ** ** He didn't have to do that for DJ... ** ** And he sure as hell didn't have to leave his comfortable life as a New York billionaire to become one of the **most lied about men in the world. ** ** ** But he did it because America deserves a fighter... ** ** **President Trump saw that** ******same****fighter in DJ.** ** ** **And today, I 'm hoping you'll ** **see that fighter in me.** ** **

D.C. politicians have worn out their welcome. ** ** Democrat or Republican, they've gotten comfortable. ** ** And when they are comfortable, they get lazy. ** ** And when they get lazy, _they get forgetful and disrespectful_. ** ** **President Trump showed me and** **my son RESPECT.** ** ** **Now it 's time for somebody in Washington D.C. to show ** **YOU some respect too.** ** ** I will _NEVER_ give up on this country. ** ** I will _NEVER_ surrender to the forces trying to bring us down. ** ** And I will _NEVER_ sell you down the river like all these crooked politicians do. ** ** I don't have a big political agenda to fill you in on. ** ** No "Ra-Ra" speech today. ** ** **I 'm just asking you to chip in $5 to my campaign so I can fight for the country and _fight for the President that has given me and my son so much to live for_.** ** ** **_Can I count on your support?_**

clean_body: | | | | | Fellow American, That's a photo of me and my son DJ. The doctor told me he had 5 months left to live. He was 6 years old at the time. He's 13 now - and he 's the most patriotic person I've ever met. I may be a whole two feet taller than DJ, but I look up to him every single day. DJ inspired me to run for Congress as a Republican. But that 's not even half of our story. Do you have 2 minutes to read the rest? My name is Theodis Daniel. Seven years ago, if you had told me President Trump would one day be swearing in my son as an honorary member of the U.S. Secret Service, I would 've never believed you. It was around that time DJ was diagnosed with a rare form of brain and spinal cancer. My world was crushed. As a single father, DJ was the shining light that made my life worth living. We went through MONTHS of treatment together... We did EVERYTHING the doctor told us to do... But to this day,I know it was God who saved my son. Fast forward seven years, and DJ's stronger than ever before. His story INSPIRED millions, and even caught the attention of President Trump. That 's when DJ's biggest dream finallycame true. (You may remember watching this on TV) At President Trump 's joint address to Congress, DJ was sworn in as an honorary member of the U.S. Secret Service. I remember DJ trying to keep a serious look on his face. After all, he vowed to protect President Trump with his life. But the irony is, it was President Trump who gave DJ a whole new reason to live. That 's why I'm running for Congress. The media wants you to believe President Trump is nothing but a "heartless, ruthless, dictator. " But I saw first hand that he is a father, grandfather, and compassionate man. He didn't have to do that for DJ... And he sure as hell didn't have to leave his comfortable life as a New York billionaire to become one of the most lied about men in the world. But he did it because America deserves a fighter... President Trump saw that ****samefighter in DJ. And today, I 'm hoping you'll see that fighter in me. D.C. politicians have worn out their welcome. Democrat or Republican, they've gotten comfortable. And when they are comfortable, they get lazy. And when they get lazy, they get forgetful and disrespectful. President Trump showed me and my son RESPECT. Now it 's time for somebody in Washington D.C. to show YOU some respect too. I will NEVER give up on this country. I will NEVER surrender to the forces trying to bring us down. And I will NEVER sell you down the river like all these crooked politicians do. I don't have a big political agenda to fill you in on. No "Ra-Ra" speech today. I 'm just asking you to chip in $5 to my campaign so I can fight for the country and fight for the President that has given me and my son so much to live for. Can I count on your support? I look forward to hearing from you. God bless. The Battle Continues, Theodis Daniel Republican for Congress (TX) P.S. Patriot, before rushing over your $5 donation, please read the kind words President Trump said about my son DJ : "Joining us in the gallery tonight is a young man who truly loves our police ...The doctors gave him five months at most to live. That was more than six years ago... Since that time, DJ and his dad have been on a quest to make his dream come true. " 3636 OLD SPANISH TRAILSTE B-1 #239HOUSTON, TX 77021

name: Yassamin Ansari
subject: I’ll be voting no on this “deal”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress
body:
Working families can’t afford this. |
---
Peter, Last night, the Senate voted to move forward on a continuing resolution. But this so-called “deal” doesn’t provide any protection for Americans’ access to affordable healthcare. It makes no effort to assist the 370,000 Arizonans who will have to pay more for their healthcare coverage. **Working families can’t afford that.** Donald Trump and House Republicans are still refusing to come to the table and negotiate on a real solution that would protect Americans’ healthcare access. And now, after stalling any negotiations and giving House Republicans almost two months of vacation, Speaker Mike Johnson is directing Republicans to come back to Congress “right now” to vote on this bogus deal. **I’m heading to DC, where I’ll be voting NO on the continuing resolution.** [I’m not backing down, Peter. body:
[1]Justice Democrats

BREAKING, Peter:

Eight corporate Senate Democrats just caved and voted to surrender our
healthcare to Republicans who plan to raise premiums for millions of
Americans to pay for Trump’s billionaire tax cuts.

Democrats held their ground for 40 days — the longest government shutdown
in American history — to stop health insurance premiums from skyrocketing
even higher than they already are for millions of working people.

Then, these eight corporate Democrats folded... for nothing in return. And
Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer let them.

It’s time for Chuck Schumer to step down as the Democratic leader in the
Senate.

This is absolutely pathetic behavior by the so-called opposition party.
Voters expected Democrats to hold the line for their basic healthcare and
food benefits — instead, they surrendered.

Every Senate Democrat who joined Republicans to pass this resolution sold
the American people out, and we should make sure they have no future in
public office.

This is what Justice Democrats has been saying from the beginning: we
don’t just need more Democrats in office — we need champions for
working-class communities who will actually fight for the people.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251110.LjyzZB&refcode=em20251110-37183&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37183%2E1203867%2EJbL3_J ]Help us clean house in the Democratic Party and support progressive,
working-class candidates taking on corporate stooges in next year’s
primary elections: rush $5 or more now!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251110.LjyzZB&refcode=em20251110-37183&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1021&akid=37183%2E1203867%2EJbL3_J ]Contribute $5

Voting blue no matter who isn’t enough,
Peter. It’s time we make that clear to the
feckless Democrats leading our party and make room for real fighters.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251110-37183-footer&t=1022&refcode2=37183_1203867_JbL3_J&akid=37183%2E1203867%2EJbL3_J

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1023&akid=37183%2E1203867%2EJbL3_J

subject: I will not back down, especially when it comes to affordable health care.

body:
| | Team, while congressional Republicans are causing chaos and hurting our families, I have not yielded. Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill, combined with their refusal to extend the ACA health care tax credits, means almost _35,000_ people in my district alone will lose their health coverage entirely, and another _23,000_ will see their costs go up. That is completely unacceptable, especially when living and raising a family is more expensive than ever. **[I am fighting to protect and expand health care access, not rip it away from our most vulnerable.
